#  Fuel Substitution in Energy Efficiency
#### VEA Phase 2 Staff Proposal
On December 1, 2025, the VEA Phase 2 Staff Proposal was released via [ALJ Ruling](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?docformat=ALL&docid=589486895).
Formal Comments are due to the R.25-04-010 Proceeding by January 6th, 2026. Reply comments are due January 16, 2026.
On the PDF of the Staff Proposal can be found here: [VEA Phase 2 Staff Proposal](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/building-decarb/vea-staff-proposal-final-draft.pdf).
#### Fuel Substitution in Energy Efficiency
In the context of energy efficiency programs, fuel substitution measures involve projects where all or a portion of the existing energy use is converted from one CPUC-regulated fuel to another CPUC-regulated fuel. Equipment powered by electricity and/or natural gas fuels and provided by a CPUC-regulated investor-owned utility or a municipal utility are eligible for inclusion in the energy efficiency portfolio as a fuel substitution measure. Fuel substitution measures are distinct from fuel switching measures. Fuel switching measures involve non-utility fuels such as propane or fuel oil. 
In order to include a fuel substitution measure in the energy efficiency portfolio, program administrators must demonstrate that the proposed measure passes the Fuel Substitution Test implemented by Decision 19-08-009. The Fuel Substitution Test has two requirements:
  1. The measure shall not increase source energy
  2. The measure shall not harm the environment


For the purpose of passing the Fuel Substitution Test, environmental harm is measured by CO2 emissions. Therefore, to be included in the portfolio, fuel substitution measures must not increase source energy or CO2 emissions compared to the baseline technology. The fuel substitution technical guidance document (linked below) provides more information on the Fuel Substitution Test. 
Decisions 19-08-009 directed Energy Division to produce a technical guidance document for fuel substitution measures. The technical guidance document includes a step-by-step guide for calculating source energy savings and CO2 offsets for fuel substitution measures. The guidance document instructs users on how to demonstrate that measures pass the Fuel Substitution Test and outlines the calculation methodology. Additionally, the guide provides information on cost effectiveness analyses and reporting requirements. The technical guidance document is a living document and will be updated periodically and in conjunction with the Integrated Distributed Energy Resources proceeding (R.14-10-003) and Integrated Resources Planning proceeding (R.16-02-007). 
The Fuel Substitution Test and the technical guidance document are applicable only to fuel substitution measures which are eligible for energy efficiency incentives and included in the program administrator's energy efficiency portfolios. The Building Decarbonization proceeding (R.19-01-011) is considering the BUILD and TECH programs, which will likely include fuel substitution technologies. Fuel Substitution activities undertaken through the Building Decarbonization proceeding are not subject to the Fuel Substitution Test. For more information on the Building Decarbonization proceeding, please visit: [https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/buildingdecarb/](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/energy-division/building-decarbonization).
For more information on fuel substitution measures, review Decision 19-08-009 and the technical guidance document linked below. The Fuel Substitution Calculator, also linked below, is an excel based tool used to perform the Fuel Substitution Test calculations. Both versions of the calculator perform the same calculations and are valid, but v.1.1 includes additional usability for deemed and custom project calculations. The technical guidance document includes a guide for using the Fuel Substitution Calculator.
## Workshops
Fuel Substitution Infrastructure Cost Market Study Webinar - 3/5/24   
Contact: William.Graswich@cpuc.ca.gov
The 2023 Viable Electric Alternative (VEA) Decision ([D. 23-04-035](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M505/K808/505808197.PDF)) directed the CPUC to conduct a statewide market study to assess the residential and commercial infrastructure costs associated with market rate customers switching from gas to electric end uses. This market study was necessary because there was no robust statewide data on this subject. The market study, conducted by Opinion Dynamics under the supervision of the CPUC, surveyed residential customers, commercial customers, and electricians. The results of this study, and the analysis of the results, will be summarized in this webinar.
  * [Market Study Report](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/building-decarb/fs-infra-ms_overall-report_20240606.pdf)
  * [Webinar recording](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/energy/Building%20Decarb/EE%20Fuel%20Substitution%20Infrastructure%20Cost%20Market%20Study%20Webinar-20240305%201801-1.mp4)
  * [Presentation slides](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/building-decarb/cpuc_fsinframs_stakeholderpresentation_2024006.pdf) (Updated 6/11/24)
  * This data [tool](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/building-decarb/fs-infra-ms-data-tool-market-rate--results-final.xlsx) provides an easy-to-use way to review and access the market studies results. (Updated 6/11/24)
    * [Methods explanation](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/building-decarb/fs-infra-ms_datatoolanalysismethods_updated-20240605.pdf) for the data tool. (Updated 6/11/24)


Combined Refrigerant Avoided Cost and Fue Substitution Calculators with Technical Guidance Presentation Webinar - 3/06/24
Contact: William.Graswich@cpuc.ca.gov
DNV will present the most recent updates to the now merged Refrigerant Avoided Cost and Fuel-Substitution Calculators as well as the accompanying Technical Guidance Document. The combined tool includes recent code updates, refrigerant GWP baselines, and many additional features designed to help stakeholders chart, understand, and target activities that will improve refrigerant GHG impacts on both deemed and custom measures.
The Calculators and Technical Guidance Document will be uploaded to the Energy Division’s Public Document Area (PDA) on 2/28/24. You can access the PDA here: [CPUC Energy Evaluation Public Comment (energydataweb.com)](https://pda.energydataweb.com/#!/#!/)
  * [Webinar recording](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/energy/Building%20Decarb/EE%20Fuel%20Substitution%20CalculatorRefrigerant%20Avoided%20Cost%20Calculator%20Update%20Webinar-20240306%202301-1.mp4)
  * [Presentation slides](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/building-decarb/cpuc_racc-fsc-technical-guidance-presentation_20240306.pptx)


Substitution Calculator Update Presentation - 10/13/22
Beta testing of the new draft calculator will occur between 10/13 and 11/14. During this time please email any comments or questions on the draft calculator or its documentation to Travis Holtby (travis.holtby@cpuc.ca.gov) and Michaela Levine (michaela.levine@ethree.com). Once we have received and addressed stakeholder commenters, we will make any necessary edits and publish the new finalized Fuel Substitution Calculator, which is planned for winter 2022.
  * [Draft Fuel Substitution Calculator v2.0](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/building-decarb/fuel-substitution-calculator-v20.xlsx)
  * [Draft Fuel Substitution Technical Guidance Guide v2](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/building-decarb/fuel-substitution-technical-guide-v2.docx)

#  Accessibility
The California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) is committed to providing information and services to everyone with care and respect. We treat all persons with courtesy and dignity regardless of position or personal characteristics and foster an inclusive environment free of bias.
Some of the steps we are taking to provide more accessible web content include, but are not limited to: engaging vendors to assist with website redesign and historical content assessment; increasing CPUC staff awareness and training on addressing accessibility issues; conversion of historical documents to an accessible format; and evaluating new and refreshed web content for compliance with evolving accessibility standards.
Some content on this website such as PDF documents might require a free Adobe Acrobat Reader. Download [Adobe Acrobat Reader DC](https://get.adobe.com/reader/).
## Certification
The undersigned certify as of May 1, 2023, that the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) is working to ensure its website complies with California Government Code Sections 11546.7, 7405 and 11135, and the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.1, or a subsequent version, published by the Web Accessibility Initiative of the World Wide Web Consortium at a minimum Level AA success criteria.  
  
Ongoing efforts include conversion of historical documents to an accessible format and engaging with third parties to address accessibility of our documents and content. The CPUC strives to ensure that its website content and services are accessible to all visitors.  
  
If there is any difficulty accessing material, please contact us by emailing webmaster@cpuc.ca.gov for correction or for information in an alternative format.  
  
/s/Ryan Dulin  
Deputy Executive Director, Internal Operations  
(Acting Chief Information Officer)  
California Public Utilities Commission  
  
/s/RACHEL PETERSON  
Executive Director  
California Public Utilities Commission  
  
Date: May 1, 2023

  3. [Privacy Policy](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/privacy-policy)


#  CPUC Privacy Policy
The California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) is committed to promoting and protecting the privacy rights of individuals as enumerated in Article 1 of the California Constitution, the Information Practices Act of 1977, and other state and federal laws.
The CPUC follows these principles in collecting and managing personal information:
The CPUC collects personal information on individuals only as allowed by law. The CPUC limits the collection of personal information to what is relevant and necessary to accomplish the lawful purpose of the CPUC. Personal information, as defined in the Information Practices Act, is information that identifies or describes an individual including, but not limited to, name, Social Security number, physical description, home address, home telephone number, education, financial matters and medical or employment history.
The CPUC does not collect home, business or e-mail addresses, or account information from persons who simply browse our Internet website. The information that the CPUC automatically collects includes the domain name or Internet Protocol address that relates to the machine used to access the CPUC’s website, the type of browser and operating system used, the date and time when the website is visited, web pages displayed, and any forms that are uploaded. Such electronically collected information is exempt from disclosure in response to requests made under the Public Records Act.
The CPUC may use session cookies in some areas of this website to improve the overall usability of the site. Cookies are small text files placed on your computer, often without your knowledge, while visiting certain sites on the Internet used to identify your computer. For example, cookies are used by some sites to greet you personally, to post a list of your favorite books to purchase, to remember what you had in your shopping basket the last time you visited. On this website, a temporary cookie may be placed on your computer for certain applications such as online complaint forms which can be sent electronically. The session cookies are used to facilitate the interaction between you and the fields in the form. The cookies are not used to collect or store information about you and do not track you if you return to our website. The cookie is temporary and will be deleted when you exit your browser. You can prevent cookies from being placed on your computer by accessing your browser's preferences menu and deleting existing cookies. There also are commercial programs available to help you manage cookies. Before taking such steps, you should be aware that some websites may not work properly if you choose to block the placement of cookies on your computer. You will need to decide whether enabling cookies outweigh privacy concerns.
The CPUC makes the best effort
  1. About the specific usage that the CPUC will make of that information.
  2. The CPUC will provide information on the authority under which the request is made.
  3. The possible disclosures the CPUC is obligated to make to other government agencies and to the public.


The CPUC uses personal information only for the specified purposes for which the information was collected, or purposes consistent with those purposes, unless the CPUC receives the consent of the subject of the information, or unless authorized by law or regulation. The CPUC does not disclose any personal information, does not make personal information available, and does not sell any electronically collected personal information, to any third-party vendor. The CPUC allows individuals who provide personal information to review the information and contest its accuracy or completeness.
The Public Records Act exists to ensure that government is open, and that the public has a right to have access to appropriate records and information possessed by state government. At the same time, there are exceptions in both state and federal law to the public’s right to access public records. These exceptions serve various needs including maintaining the privacy of individuals. In the event of a conflict between this Policy and the Public Records Act, the Information Practices Act, or other law governing the disclosure of records, the applicable law will control. 
The CPUC uses information security safeguards. The CPUC takes reasonable precautions to protect the personal information on individuals collected or maintained by the CPUC against loss, unauthorized access, use, modification, or disclosure. The CPUC has security measures in place to protect against loss, unauthorized access, use, modification, or disclosure of the information under its control. Information that is physically located within the CPUC is protected by various security measures which may include the following: on our Web sites, the CPUC uses encryption software to protect the security of individuals’ personal information during transmission of such information throughout the CPUC’s Web sites. Personal information is stored in secure locations. CPUC staff are trained on procedures for the management and release of personal information, and access to personal information is limited to those staff whose work requires it. Confidential information is destroyed according to the CPUC’s records retention schedules. The CPUC conducts periodic audits to ensure that proper information management policies and procedures are being followed.
The CPUC will perform a Privacy Threshold Assessment (PTA) and, if required, a Privacy Impact Assessment (PTI), for any systems that collect personal information data, to ensure that the CPUC is compliant with any applicable privacy laws, polices, regulations, and government or industry standards.
Users have the right to have their electronically collected personal information be deleted. A user may request that his/her electronically collected personal information be deleted by emailing the request to privacy@cpuc.ca.gov.
This policy is effective 01/04/2023 and shall be reviewed annually or as required. Please note that our Privacy Policy reflects current CPUC business practices and changes will be reflected on the website.
If you have further questions, comments, or complaints about the CPUC privacy policy or policy compliance, you may contact:
California Public Utilities Commission  
ATTN: Privacy Officer  
505 Van Ness  
San Francisco, CA 94102  
privacy@cpuc.ca.gov  
415-696-7400
See also "[Conditions of use](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/conditions-of-use)" for further information on how CPUC adheres to the Privacy Policies set out by the State of California.

#  File a Complaint
The CPUC can help you file a complaint against a utility or road transportation service (such as a limousine, airport shuttle, or charter bus company). 
## [ Public Safety Complaint ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/file-a-complaint/public-safety-complaint)
Let us know about public safety issues related to gas, electric, cable, telephone, water, or mobile home park utilities. 
## [ Whistleblower Complaint ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/file-a-complaint/whistleblower-complaint)
As an employee, contractor, or subcontractor of a company regulated by the Commission, you may report suspected unsafe, unlawful, or dishonest activity. 
## [ Utility Complaint ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/file-a-complaint/utility-complaint)
If you cannot resolve the problem after talking with the utility’s customer service, you may file an informal complaint with us. 
## [ Formal Complaint ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/file-a-complaint/filing-a-formal-complaint)
Through the formal complaint procedure, the CPUC can order your utility to take corrective action on various formal complaints. 
## [ Transportation Complaint ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/file-a-complaint/transportation-complaint)
If you cannot resolve the problem after talking with the transportation company, you may file a complaint with us. 
## [ Unauthorized Service Changes ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/file-a-complaint/unauthorized-service-charges)
If a company switched without permission (slamming) or charged for things you didn’t order (cramming), file a complaint. 
## [ Complaints About the CPUC ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/file-a-complaint/complaints-about-cpuc)
Let the Public Advisor know about your complaint about the CPUC, and determine how we can improve. 
## [ Comment on a CPUC Proceeding ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/news-and-public-information-office/public-advisors-office/providing-public-comments-at-the-cpuc)
The CPUC wants to hear from you! We encourage comments from the public on issues and proceedings to help us reach informed decisions. 
## [ Check Case Status ](https://cims.cpuc.ca.gov/complaints/)
Check the status of your complaint 
##  How can we help?
Emergency? **Call 911**

#  Energy Efficiency
California has led the nation in energy efficiency programs since the 1970s. Due to the state’s long standing efficiency programs, per capita energy use has remained flat, while the rest of the US has increased by about 33 percent. 
The CPUC regulates investor-owned utility (IOU) ratepayer-funded energy efficiency programs. The CPUC works with the IOUs, other portfolio administrators, and vendors to develop programs and measures to transform technology markets within California using ratepayer funds.
  * [CPUC's role in regulating Energy Efficiency](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/cpucs-role-in-energy-efficiency-programs)


## Proceeding Information
  * [Rulemaking R.25-04-010](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R2504010) - The new Energy Efficiency proceeding for oversight of energy efficiency portfolios, policies, programs and evaluation.
  * [Rulemaking R.13-11-005](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:15906284397852::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R1311005) - Recently closed Energy Efficiency proceeding.
  * [Application A.22-02-005](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:A2202005) et al. - Applications from nine EE Portfolio Administrators requesting approval of strategies, programs and budgets for 2024-2031.
  * Major Recent Decisions: 
    * [D.25-11-023](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M588/K645/588645495.PDF): Decision approving initial tranche of energy efficiency market transformation initiatives
    * [D.25-08-034](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M579/K006/579006275.PDF): Energy Efficiency Goals for 2026-2037
    * [D.23-06-055](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?DocFormat=ALL&DocID=512907396): Authorization of Energy Efficiency Strategies, Budgets, and Portfolios for 2024-2031 and 2028-2031
    * [D.23-04-035](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M505/K808/505808197.PDF): (VEA) - Viable Electric Alternative Decision
    * [D.21-05-031](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M385/K864/385864616.PDF): Established Total System Benefit (TSB) as the goals metric and assesses energy efficiency potential and goals policy decision and modification of portfolio approval and oversight process.
    * [D.19-12-021](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?DocFormat=ALL&DocID=321507615): Frameworks for Energy Efficiency Regional Energy Networks and Market Transformation
    * [D.18-05-041](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M215/K706/215706139.PDF): Energy Efficiency-Business Plan Decision (2018-2025)
  * For more proceeding information, see [Energy Efficiency Procurement and Solicitations](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-proceeding-activity)

Key Data and Reports:
  * [CPUC's California Energy Data and Reporting System (CEDARs)](https://cedars.sound-data.com/) - for viewing up-to-date savings, budgets, expenditures, and cost effectiveness of California's EE programs
  * [California Electronic Technical Resource Manual (eTRM)](https://www.caetrm.com/login/?next=/) - an online application that serves a repository for all statewide deemed measures for California
  * [Evaluation Studies Public Document Search](https://pda.energydataweb.com/#!/#!/) - view current master evaluation plan, search research plans, draft reports, and provide comments on active documents
  * [CalMAC](https://www.calmac.org/search.asp) - California Measurement Advisory Council (CALMAC) - library of program evaluation and research reports on EE and other clean energy programs


## Energy Efficiency Resources:
  * [2020 EE Policy Manual](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/eepolicymanualrevised-march-20-2020-b.pdf)
  * [Portfolio Administrator-Filed Reports](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-reporting)


  * [Rolling Portfolio Program Guidance](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance)
  * [Links to Portfolio Administrators](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-program-implementers)
  * [Publications, Evaluations, and Reports](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-publications)
  * Response to Recommendation (RTR) Materials 
    * [RTR Tracker](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rtr-response-to-recommendation-tracker.xlsx) - spreadsheet tracking RTR due dates and submittals 
    * [RTR Process ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rtr-processv2.docx)- document detailing the RTR communication and follow-up process
    * [RTR Template](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rtr-template.xlsx) - example RTR document (unfilled)


## Information for Consumers
If you are looking for EE Programs for your home, business or property, please see the webpage of the EE Program Administrators that serve your area:
  * [Pacific Gas & Electric EE Programs](https://www.pge.com/en/save-energy-and-money/energy-saving-programs.html) (serves Northern California)
  * [Marin Clean Energy Programs](https://www.mcecleanenergy.org/customer-programs/) (serves Contra Costa, Marin, Napa, and Solano Counties)
  * [Bay Area Regional Energy Network](https://www.bayren.org/programs-rebates) (serves Bay Area)
  * [Tri County Regional Energy Network](https://www.3c-ren.org/) (serves San Luis Obispo, Santa Barbara, and Ventura Counties) 
  * [Northern Rural Energy Network](https://northernren.org/) (serves Alpine, Amador, Butte, Calaveras, El Dorado, Humboldt, Lake, Lassen, Mendocino, Mariposa, Nevada, Placer, Plumas, Sierra, Sutter, Tuolumne, and Yuba Counties) 
  * [Central Rural Energy Network](https://www.ccrren.org/) (serves Fresno, Inyo, Kern, Kings, Madera, Merced, Mono, Monterey, San Benito, San Joaquin, San Luis Obispo, Stanislaus, and Tulare Counties)
  * [Southern California Edison](https://www.sce.com/clean-energy-efficiency) (serves Southern California)
  * [Southern California Gas Programs](https://www.socalgas.com/save-money-and-energy) (serves Southern California)
  * [Southern California Regional Energy Network](https://socalren.org/) (serves Southern California)
  * [San Diego Gas and Electric Programs](https://www.sdge.com/node/28306) (serves San Diego County and Southern Orange County)
  * San Diego Regional Energy Network


If you have been contacted by a CPUC evaluation contractor and wish to verify that the request is legitimate, click[](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/General.aspx?id=4373 "Energy Efficiency Program Implementers") to access [current active surveys](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/valid-survey).
  * [New Vendor Inquiries](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/c/5297-cpucwebsiteideationcollateral-ioufinaldraft10172013-clean-links.pdf "Submitting Energy Efficiency Program Proposals") - IOU processes for considering new energy efficiency technology and program ideas 
  * [Income Qualified Assistance Programs](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/financial-assistance-savings-and-discounts) - Assistance for qualified households to save energy and reduce monthly bills.


Archive Energy Efficiency page - click [here](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/Error-Pages/404?item=web%3a%7bAF3381DF-5368-4F91-812E-CCFBD035A60D%7d%40en)

#  Power Outage Maps
The State’s investor-owned electric utilities, Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E), Southern California Edison (SCE), San Diego Gas & Electric (SDG&E), Bear Valley Electric Service, Liberty Utilities and PacifiCorp, may shut off electric power, referred to as “de-energization” or Public Safety Power Shut-offs, to protect public safety. 
## [ PG&E ](https://pgealerts.alerts.pge.com/outages/map/)
Learn more about the PG&E Public Safety Power Shut Off. View a current outage map as well as more information. 
## [ San Diego Gas & Electric ](https://www.sdge.com/residential/customer-service/outage-center/outage-map)
Learn more about the San Diego Gas & Electric Public Safety Power Shutoff and view a current outage map. 
## [ Southern California Edison ](https://www.sce.com/outage-center/check-outage-status)
Learn more about the Southern California Edison Public Safety Power Shutoff and view a current outage map. 
## [ Bear Valley Electric Service ](https://www.bvesinc.com/about/news)
Learn more about the Bear Valley Electric Service Public Safety Power Shut Off and view current outage information. 
## [ Liberty Utilities ](https://california.libertyutilities.com/truckee/residential/emergencies/electrical/outages-map.html)
Learn more about the Liberty Utilities Public Safety Power Shut Off. View a current outage map as well for more information. 
## [ PacifiCorp ](https://www.pacificpower.net/about/newsroom/news-releases/outage-update.html)
Learn more about the PacifiCorp Public Safety Power Shut Off and view current outage information. 
##  More information
### [ Cooling Centers ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/cooling-centers)
A Cooling Center provides a place for you and your family to go to cool down during hot summer days. 
### [ De-Energization ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/psps)
Electric power shut-offs, referred to as “de-energization” or Public Safety Power Shut-offs, aim to protect public safety under California law. 
### [ Preparing for a Power Outage ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/preparing-for-a-power-outage)
We created a checklist to prepare you and your family if there is a power outage. 
### [ Outage Reporting for Power Plants ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/power-outage-maps/web-based-power-plant-outage)
Power plants can report outages directly to us. Customers should report outages on their utility websites. 
### [ Electric Reliability and Safety ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/infrastructure/electric-reliability)
The CPUC enforces rules and regulations to ensure that power plant operators and utility companies run a safe and reliable electric or communication system.

  6. [Clean Energy Financing](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/clean-energy-financing)


#  Clean Energy Financing
The Commission instituted Rulemaking R. 20-08-022 to examine options to assist electricity and natural gas customers with investments in residential and commercial buildings and at industrial and agricultural sites designed to decrease energy use, reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, and/or produce clean energy to support customers' on-site needs. 
This rulemaking is designed specifically to examine options that encourage larger-scale and deeper investments in one or more clean energy resources at customer sites. In addition, this rule-making will examine options for multiple sources of funding by combining and leveraging ratepayer funds with private financing to support these more comprehensive investments. 
Order Instituting Rulemaking [R. 20-08-022](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M346/K361/346361154.PDF)
  * August 10, 2023: [CPUC Expands On-Bill Financing Options for Non-Residential Energy Utility Customers](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/news-and-updates/all-news/cpuc-expands-on-bill-financing-options-for-non-residential-energy-utility-customers-2023) (CPUC Press Release)

#  Industries and Topics
We regulate utility companies and infrastructure in California. 
## [ Electrical Energy ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy)
Learn more about electric utility consumer programs, regulatory services, news, and events. 
## [ Internet and Phone ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/internet-and-phone)
We regulate and enforce policies for consumer protection in the communications industry. Learn more about regulatory services and consumer programs. 
## [ Natural Gas ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/natural-gas)
Learn more about natural gas utility consumer programs, regulatory services, news, and events. 
## [ Rail Safety ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/rail-safety-division)
We regulate rail safety including freight, inter-city and commuter railroads, rail transit and rail crossings. 
## [ Transportation ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/transportation)
Learn more about how we enforce safety of passenger carriers like limousines, charter buses, ferries, transportation network companies, such as Uber or Lyft through regulatory services. 
## [ Water ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/water)
We investigate water system service quality issues, analyze, and process utility rate change requests. 
##  Key Issues and Topics
### [ Affordability ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/affordability)
Learn about Proceeding R.18-07-006, which assesses the impacts on affordability of individual CPUC proceedings and utility rate requests. 
### [ Aliso Canyon ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/regulatory-services/safety/gas-safety-and-reliability-branch/aliso-canyon-well-failure)
Our investigation and analysis of the Aliso Canyon well failure and more information on the incident. 
### [ COVID-19 Relief ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/news-and-updates/newsroom/covid-19)
Find information on what the CPUC and utilities under its jurisdiction are doing to support communities during these challenging times. 
### [ CPUC Tribal Office ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/office-of-the-tribal-advisor)
The CPUC is committed to incorporating Tribal concerns into the development of regulations, and policies through engagement in discussion and consultation with Tribes. 
### [ Environmental and Social Justice Action Plan ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/news-and-updates/newsroom/environmental-and-social-justice-action-plan)
The ESJ Action Plan is a commitment to advancing CPUC decisions and programs that strive to provide everyone across the state with consumer protections and other benefits. 
### [ PG&E ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/pge)
Get the latest information on how we are working with PG&E and protecting their consumers. 
### [ Prison Phone Calls ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/internet-and-phone/rulemaking-considering-regulation-of-telecommunication-services-used-by-incarcerated-people)
Learn more about what we are doing to consider the regulation of telecommunication services used by incarcerated people. 
### [ Public Safety Power Shutoffs ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/psps)
Learn what we are doing to minimize impacts of utility PSPS events. 
###  Low Cost Internet Plans
Find opportunities available in your neighborhood for low cost internet plans. 
### [ Broadband Infrastructure Deployment ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/internet-and-phone/broadband-infrastructure-deployment)
Learn about the proceeding to to accelerate the deployment of and access to quality, affordable internet for all Californians. 
### [ Solar Consumer Protection Guide ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/customer-generation/california-solar-consumer-protection-guide)
This guide provides important information to homeowners thinking of going solar. 
### [ Summer Reliability ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/summer-2021-reliability)
This page details efforts the CPUC is taking to ensure sufficient resources are available to meet California’s electricity demand for the summer. 
##  How can we help?
Emergency? **Call 911**

#  CPUC's Role in Energy Efficiency Programs
The CPUC oversees and approves the energy efficiency programs funded by charges established in:
**Public Utilities Code Section 381 (Public Goods Charge):**
  * Created by Assembly Bill (AB) 1890 in September 1996
  * Established a Public Goods Charge (PGC) that consumers pay on electricity consumption for cost-effective energy efficiency, renewable technologies, and public interest research
  * In September 2000, AB 995 became law, extending the electric PGC through January 1, 2012


**Public Utilities Code Section 890 – 900 (Gas Surcharge):**
  * Created by AB 1002 in February 1999
  * Established a natural gas surcharge to fund cost-effective energy efficiency and other public purpose programs


**Public Utilities Code Section 399.15 (b)/379.5:**
  * Created by AB 970 in September 2000 for expanded energy efficiency programs plus new load control and distributed generation initiatives
  * SBx1-5 (April 2001):
  * Expanded appliance, lighting, and HVAC rebates, appliance recycling, and oil and gas efficiency programs

#  Custom Projects Review Home Page
Custom Projects Review Home Page   
[Custom Projects Review Guidance Page](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents)   
[Custom Projects Stakeholder Engagement Group](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-stakeholder-engagement-page)
## Custom Projects Review
**What is Custom Project Review?**
Custom measures and projects are site-specific energy efficiency projects. Custom Projects require unique calculations for each project, as they do not rely on fixed DEER or workpaper values. For custom measures and projects, the energy efficiency savings estimated values cannot be forecasted in advance, since the preliminary forecasted values are not known until the project is identified. The forecast energy efficiency savings values are not developed until the project is completed. Custom Programs include projects in Commercial, Residential, Industrial & Agricultural Sectors.
**Why do we do Custom Project Review?**
In 2009 the CPUC began conducting evaluations of Custom Projects that were receiving rate-payer incentives and found that gross and net energy efficiency savings on custom projects were dropping from year to year, and that free-ridership (projects receiving rate-payer incentives even though the customer would have switched to energy efficient products without the incentive) was also increasing to around 40%-50%. The CPUC’s evaluations also found areas where policy is not appropriately implemented and that assumptions, methods and data utilized are not always the most appropriate. Because of these factors, the CPUC started its Custom Project Review Process to improve the program administrators’ internal project review process.
**The objectives of Custom Project Review are:**
  1. Improve the accuracy and reliability of net efficiency savings and cost effectiveness estimates to meet net efficiency goals.
  2. Ensure ratepayer funded incentives achieve real net incremental efficiency savings.
  3. Foster ongoing improvements to the utilities' review activities.


**Custom Project Review Process**
Commission Decision [11-07-030](https://file.ac/6XsdKwUyhqo/) establishes a clear process by which forecasted energy efficiency savings estimates from custom measures and projects are reviewed. Under this Decision, twice a month utilities submit a list of new Custom Project applications that they have already reviewed that are planned within their service territories. From these bimonthly submissions, the CPUC selects a sample of projects to review. [Public Utilities Code](http://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billNavClient.xhtml?bill_id=201720180SB1131 "Public Utilities Code") 381.2 (per Senate Bill (SB) 1131) mandates that the CPUC may take no longer than 30 days to review a project that falls under that law. The CPUC must complete its review of a project with a complete project package within 30 days and will issue a disposition on the project, either approving it, approving it with recommendations, or rejecting it. Rejected projects may be revised and resubmitted for a new review. If the disposition for a project under SB 1131 exceeds the 30 days then the CPUC review becomes advisory-only, and the CPUC's recommendations are not required to be followed.
![](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/images/custom-projects-review-process.png)
The CPUC also reviews Custom Projects that are not covered by SB 1131. Projects in this category can include normalized meter energy consumption (NMEC) programs, strategic energy management (SEM) program, and high opportunity program and projects (HOPP). These review dispositions are advisory-only.

#  Demand Side Management
## About DER
The Commission oversees programs and market mechanisms to help customer manage their energy use. Customers have a wide range of “distributed energy resources” (DERs) to choose from. DERs are “distribution-connected distributed generation resources such as energy efficiency, demand response, customer generation (e.g., rooftop solar), energy storage, alternative fuel vehicles (e.g., electric vehicles), and water-energy conservation." A diverse portfolio of DER solutions is available to customers through programs, tariffs, and procurements run directly by the utility of third-party implementers.
## Learn More
Distributed Energy Resources (DER) technologies and strategies include the following examples:
  * Customer-owned generation, such as rooftop solar and energy storage
  * Demand Response, which provides incentives to shift energy usage to times when demand on the grid has the highest value and can avoid infrastructure investment
  * Energy Efficiency which describes many strategies to reduce energy use, including efficient lighting, HVAC, appliances, and other items. 


Customers wanting to learn more about how to best manage energy use in their homes or businesses are invited to visit the consumer information site, [Energy Upgrade California.](http://www.energyupgradeca.org/ "Energy Upgrade California")
Since 2007, the Commission has guided investor-owned utilities to provide efficient and sensible ways of making energy management decisions easier for their customers. Decision 07-10-032 directs the utilities to “integrate customer demand-side programs, such as energy efficiency, self-generation, advanced metering, and demand response, in a coherent and efficient manner."
The Commission develops policy for these activities through proceedings dedicated to each individual DER (e.g., energy efficiency or demand response), as well as proceedings involving multiple DERs. The current Integrated Distributed Energy Resources (IDER) proceeding, Rulemaking 14-10-003, and the Distributed Resource Plan (DRP) proceeding Rulemaking 14-08-013 seek to remove barriers, ensure consistent policy, and optimize utility investments and strategies to promote cost-effective customer DER adoption. 
**Distributed Energy Resource Programs are organized into the following sections:**
  * [California Solar Initiative (CSI)](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/california-solar-initiative)
  * [Net Energy Metering](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/customer-generation)
  * [Self-Generation Incentive Program](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/self-generation-incentive-program)
  * [Solar on Multifamily Affordable Housing (SOMAH)](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/customer-generation/somah)
  * [Solar in Disadvantaged Communities](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/customer-generation/solar-in-disadvantaged-communities)
  * [Customer Generation Program Evaluations](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/customer-generation-evaluation)
  * [Contact Us](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/customer-generation-and-storage-contact-information)

#  Energy Efficiency
California has led the nation in energy efficiency programs since the 1970s. Due to the state’s long standing efficiency programs, per capita energy use has remained flat, while the rest of the US has increased by about 33 percent. 
The CPUC regulates investor-owned utility (IOU) ratepayer-funded energy efficiency programs. The CPUC works with the IOUs, other portfolio administrators, and vendors to develop programs and measures to transform technology markets within California using ratepayer funds.
  * [CPUC's role in regulating Energy Efficiency](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/cpucs-role-in-energy-efficiency-programs)


## Proceeding Information
  * [Rulemaking R.25-04-010](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R2504010) - The new Energy Efficiency proceeding for oversight of energy efficiency portfolios, policies, programs and evaluation.
  * [Rulemaking R.13-11-005](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:15906284397852::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R1311005) - Recently closed Energy Efficiency proceeding.
  * [Application A.22-02-005](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:A2202005) et al. - Applications from nine EE Portfolio Administrators requesting approval of strategies, programs and budgets for 2024-2031.
  * Major Recent Decisions: 
    * [D.25-11-023](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M588/K645/588645495.PDF): Decision approving initial tranche of energy efficiency market transformation initiatives
    * [D.25-08-034](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M579/K006/579006275.PDF): Energy Efficiency Goals for 2026-2037
    * [D.23-06-055](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?DocFormat=ALL&DocID=512907396): Authorization of Energy Efficiency Strategies, Budgets, and Portfolios for 2024-2031 and 2028-2031
    * [D.23-04-035](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M505/K808/505808197.PDF): (VEA) - Viable Electric Alternative Decision
    * [D.21-05-031](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M385/K864/385864616.PDF): Established Total System Benefit (TSB) as the goals metric and assesses energy efficiency potential and goals policy decision and modification of portfolio approval and oversight process.
    * [D.19-12-021](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?DocFormat=ALL&DocID=321507615): Frameworks for Energy Efficiency Regional Energy Networks and Market Transformation
    * [D.18-05-041](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M215/K706/215706139.PDF): Energy Efficiency-Business Plan Decision (2018-2025)
  * For more proceeding information, see [Energy Efficiency Procurement and Solicitations](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-proceeding-activity)

Key Data and Reports:
  * [CPUC's California Energy Data and Reporting System (CEDARs)](https://cedars.sound-data.com/) - for viewing up-to-date savings, budgets, expenditures, and cost effectiveness of California's EE programs
  * [California Electronic Technical Resource Manual (eTRM)](https://www.caetrm.com/login/?next=/) - an online application that serves a repository for all statewide deemed measures for California
  * [Evaluation Studies Public Document Search](https://pda.energydataweb.com/#!/#!/) - view current master evaluation plan, search research plans, draft reports, and provide comments on active documents
  * [CalMAC](https://www.calmac.org/search.asp) - California Measurement Advisory Council (CALMAC) - library of program evaluation and research reports on EE and other clean energy programs


## Energy Efficiency Resources:
  * [2020 EE Policy Manual](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/eepolicymanualrevised-march-20-2020-b.pdf)
  * [Portfolio Administrator-Filed Reports](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-reporting)


  * [Rolling Portfolio Program Guidance](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance)
  * [Links to Portfolio Administrators](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-program-implementers)
  * [Publications, Evaluations, and Reports](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-publications)
  * Response to Recommendation (RTR) Materials 
    * [RTR Tracker](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rtr-response-to-recommendation-tracker.xlsx) - spreadsheet tracking RTR due dates and submittals 
    * [RTR Process ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rtr-processv2.docx)- document detailing the RTR communication and follow-up process
    * [RTR Template](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rtr-template.xlsx) - example RTR document (unfilled)


## Information for Consumers
If you are looking for EE Programs for your home, business or property, please see the webpage of the EE Program Administrators that serve your area:
  * [Pacific Gas & Electric EE Programs](https://www.pge.com/en/save-energy-and-money/energy-saving-programs.html) (serves Northern California)
  * [Marin Clean Energy Programs](https://www.mcecleanenergy.org/customer-programs/) (serves Contra Costa, Marin, Napa, and Solano Counties)
  * [Bay Area Regional Energy Network](https://www.bayren.org/programs-rebates) (serves Bay Area)
  * [Tri County Regional Energy Network](https://www.3c-ren.org/) (serves San Luis Obispo, Santa Barbara, and Ventura Counties) 
  * [Northern Rural Energy Network](https://northernren.org/) (serves Alpine, Amador, Butte, Calaveras, El Dorado, Humboldt, Lake, Lassen, Mendocino, Mariposa, Nevada, Placer, Plumas, Sierra, Sutter, Tuolumne, and Yuba Counties) 
  * [Central Rural Energy Network](https://www.ccrren.org/) (serves Fresno, Inyo, Kern, Kings, Madera, Merced, Mono, Monterey, San Benito, San Joaquin, San Luis Obispo, Stanislaus, and Tulare Counties)
  * [Southern California Edison](https://www.sce.com/clean-energy-efficiency) (serves Southern California)
  * [Southern California Gas Programs](https://www.socalgas.com/save-money-and-energy) (serves Southern California)
  * [Southern California Regional Energy Network](https://socalren.org/) (serves Southern California)
  * [San Diego Gas and Electric Programs](https://www.sdge.com/node/28306) (serves San Diego County and Southern Orange County)
  * San Diego Regional Energy Network


If you have been contacted by a CPUC evaluation contractor and wish to verify that the request is legitimate, click[](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/General.aspx?id=4373 "Energy Efficiency Program Implementers") to access [current active surveys](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/valid-survey).
  * [New Vendor Inquiries](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/c/5297-cpucwebsiteideationcollateral-ioufinaldraft10172013-clean-links.pdf "Submitting Energy Efficiency Program Proposals") - IOU processes for considering new energy efficiency technology and program ideas 
  * [Income Qualified Assistance Programs](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/financial-assistance-savings-and-discounts) - Assistance for qualified households to save energy and reduce monthly bills.

#  DER Cost-Effectiveness
Avoided Cost Calculator (ACC)
The primary benefits of demand-side resources are the avoided costs related to generation and distribution of energy. The avoided costs of electricity are modeled based on the following components: generation energy, generation capacity, ancillary services, transmission and distribution capacity, and decarbonization policy compliance. The Avoided Cost Calculator was established in 2005 and is updated biennially to improve the accuracy of how the benefits of demand-side resources are calculated. The current ACC update was completed in 2024:
**Current Adopted (2024) ACC**
  * [2024 ACC Documentation v1b](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/demand-side-management/acc-models-latest-version/2024-acc-documentation-v1b_clean_posted_nowm.pdf)
  * [2024 ACC Gas Model v1b October Update](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/demand-side-management/acc-models-latest-version/2024-acc-gas-model-v1b_october-update_ver2.xlsx)
  * [2024 ACC Electric Model v1b](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/demand-side-management/acc-models-latest-version/2024-acc-electric-model-v1b.xlsb)
  * [2024 ACC Integrated Calculation Inputs v1b](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/demand-side-management/acc-models-latest-version/2024-acc-integrated-calculation-inputs-v1bzip.zip)
  * [2024 ACC Integrated Calculation Model](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/demand-side-management/acc-models-latest-version/integrated-calculation-model.zip)

  * [2024 SERVM TMY Dispatch and Price Results](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/Final_TMY_data.csv)
  * [2021-2022 CAISO_SERVM price and IMHR backcasting results](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/demand-response/cost-effectiveness/2021_2022_caisovsservmprices_imhr.xlsx)

**2026 (Upcoming) ACC Update (R.22-11-013, Track 1)**
On 10 April 2025, a workshop was held that covered Guiding Principles for the ACC, how to best address equity concerns in DER cost-effectiveness, and changes to the biennials update process.
  * [2025 ACC Workshop Slides](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/ider-cost-effectiveness/april-2025-acc-workshop_ver2.pdf)

  * [2025 ACC Workshop Transcript](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/ider-cost-effectiveness/april-2025-acc_workshop_transcript.docx)


On 22 August 2025, a webinar was held that covered the draft research plans for the Avoided Transmission and Distribution Costs Study.
  * [T&D Study Webinar Slides](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/ider-cost-effectiveness/td-study-webinar-slide-deck.pdf)
  * [T&D Study Webinar Recording](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/ider-cost-effectiveness/avoided-transmission-and-distribution-costs-study-webinar-20250822-2344-1.mp4)


[D.25-11-005](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M588/K680/588680206.PDF): On 25 November 2025, a decision was approved that revised the biennial update process for the ACC.
**2024 ACC Update (R.22-11-013, Track 1)**
Full documentation available above.
On 8 August 2023, a [ruling](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Efile/G000/M516/K781/516781524.PDF) was issued which included CPUC Energy Division's [Staff Proposal](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Efile/G000/M516/K712/516712053.PDF) for 2024 updates to the Avoided Cost Calculator. On 16 August 2023, Energy Division hosted a public workshop on the staff proposal. Here you can find the [slides](https://willdan.app.box.com/s/xilt6jxoza7cm63itv50jw80rtywtnea/file/1283297958089), a [recording](https://willdan.app.box.com/s/xilt6jxoza7cm63itv50jw80rtywtnea/file/1283288651439), [Q&A function text](https://willdan.app.box.com/s/xilt6jxoza7cm63itv50jw80rtywtnea/file/1283288284004), and a [transcript](https://willdan.app.box.com/s/xilt6jxoza7cm63itv50jw80rtywtnea/file/1283297482888) from the workshop. Documents relevant to this proceeding can be found on the [R.22-11-013 docket](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R2211013).
On 1 August 2024, [D.24-08-007](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M537/K784/537784099.PDF) was issued.
On 23 July 2024, the ACC Draft Calculator Workshop was held.
  * [2024 ACC Draft Calculator Workshop Slides](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/demand-response/cost-effectiveness/2024-draft-acc-workshop---final.pdf)
  * [2024 ACC Draft Calculator Workshop Recording](https://youtu.be/1Ai_0hnwDKQ)
  * [2024 ACC Draft Calculator Workshop Transcript](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/demand-response/cost-effectiveness/2024-acc-draft-calculator-workshop-20240723.docx)


On 13 November 2024, Resolution E-5328 was adopted in accordance with [D.24-08-007](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M537/K784/537784099.PDF). 
## Societal Cost Test
In February 2017, a [ruling](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Efile/G000/M173/K203/173203676.PDFhttps://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Efile/G000/M173/K203/173203676.PDF) within R.14-10-003 introduced a [staff proposal](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Efile/G000/M175/K295/175295886.PDF%20) recommending the adoption of a Societal Cost Test. [D.19-05-019](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M293/K833/293833387.PDF%20) directed the testing of specific societal cost values within the IRP proceeding (R.16-02-007). In January 2022, the "[Societal Cost Test Impact Evaluation](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/integrated-resource-plan-and-long-term-procurement-plan-irp-ltpp/2019-2020-irp-events-and-materials/societal_cost_test_impact_evaluation.pdf)" was published. In February 2023, a [ruling](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Efile/G000/M502/K201/502201444.PDF) within R.22-11-013 proceeding seeking comments on the adoption of a Societal Cost Test was published. On 11 July 2024, the Societal Cost Test was adopted by the Commission in [D.24-07-015](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M535/K822/535822173.PDF).
**Air Quality Adder**
In March 2018, a [ruling](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Efile/G000/M212/K023/212023660.PDF) in R.14-10-003 issued a Staff Proposal entitled "Distributed Energy Resource Cost-Effectiveness Evaluation: Further Recommendation on the Societal Cost Test." The data used to determine the Interim Air Quality Adder discussed in this Staff Proposal can be found [here](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/AQPublic.xlsx "here").
In January 2022, the ["Quantifying the Air Quality impacts of Decarbonization and Distributed Energy Programs in California"](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/ider-cost-effectiveness/aqi2022.pdf) report was published.
## Standard Practice Manual 
The foundation of cost-effectiveness analysis for all demand-side resources is based in the [Standard Practice Manual](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/uploadedfiles/cpuc_public_website/content/utilities_and_industries/energy_-_electricity_and_natural_gas/cpuc-standard-practice-manual.pdf "Standard Practice Manual"). The Standard Practice Manual contains the Commission’s method of evaluating energy saving investments using various cost-effectiveness tests. The four tests described in the Standard Practice Manual (i.e., the Total Resource Cost (TRC), Program Administrator Cost (PAC), Ratepayer Impact Measure (RIM), and Participant Cost Test (PCT)) assess the costs and benefits of demand-side resource programs from different stakeholder perspectives, including participants and non-participants. The specific tests and the applications of those tests varies among the resources.
**All demand-side resources undergo a cost-effectiveness analysis**
  * [Energy Efficiency](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency)
  * [Demand Response](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/electric-costs/demand-response-dr)
  * [Low Income/Energy Savings Assistance Program (ESAP)](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/financial-assistance-savings-and-discounts)
  * [Customer-Sited Renewable Energy Generation (e.g. NEM/NBT)](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/customer-generation)


**For additional background on cost-effectiveness:**
  * [Cost-effectiveness Training (6 March 2015) Recording](ftp://ftp2.cpuc.ca.gov/audiovisualfiles/Basics_of_Cost%20Effectiveness-201503061650-1.arf "Cost-effectivesness training \(3/6/15\) Recording")
  * [Cost-effectiveness Training (6 March 2015) Slides](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/c/7764-costeffectivenessandcostbenefitbasicsapril2014.pdf "Cost-effectivesness training \(3/6/15\) Powerpoint")
  * [Overview of the DER Cost-effectiveness Process](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/ider-cost-effectiveness/cost-effectiveness-brief-overview.docx)

  * The 2022 updates to the ACC were adopted in [Resolution E-5228](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M497/K155/497155265.PDF)[D.22-05-002](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M474/K624/474624547.PDF)[R.14-10-003](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R1410003) proceeding. 


**2021 ACC Update**
  * [2021 ACC Documentation](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/2021%20ACC%20Documentation%20v1b.pdf)
  * [2021 ACC Electric Model](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/2021%20ACC%20Electric%20Model%20v1b.xlsb)
  * [2021 ACC Gas Model](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/2021%20ACC%20Gas%20Model%20v1b.xlsb)
  * [2021 ACC Net Cone](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/2021%20ACC%20Net%20Cone%20v1b.xlsb)
  * [2021 ACC SERVM Prices](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/2021%20ACC%20SERVM%20Prices%20v1b.xlsb)
  * [SERVM dispatch sample output 2029](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/SERVM%20dispatch%20sample%20output%202029%20week%2040.csv)
  * [SERVM dispatch sample output 2023](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/SERVM%20dispatch%20sample%20output%202023%20week%2016.csv)
  * [2021 Refrigerant Calculator](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/2021%20ACC%20Refrigerant%20Calculator%20v1a.xlsx)
  * [2021 Storage Dispatch](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/Storage%20Dispatch%20Zipped.zip)
  * Additional data files, as well as copies of the above files, can be found at [here](https://www.ethree.com/cpuc-acc-downloads-page/).
  * The 2021 updates to the ACC were adopted in [Resolution E-5150](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M389/K955/389955728.PDF) in accordance with [D.19-05-019](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M293/K833/293833387.PDF) in the [R.14-10-003](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R1410003) proceeding.


**2020 ACC Update**
  * 6-8 May 2020 Webinar


  * [2020 ACC Update - GHG Value and Emission](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/2020%20ACC%20Update%20-%20GHG%20Value%20and%20Emissions.mp4 "2020 ACC Update - GHG Value and Emission") Webinar Recording
  * [2020 ACC Update - SERVM Prices and Net CONE](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/2020%20ACC%20Update%20-%20SERVM%20Prices%20and%20Net%20CONE.mp4) Webinar Recording
  * [CPUC ACC Webinar - Transmission and Distribution](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/CPUC%20ACC%20Webinar%20-%20Transmission%20and%20Distribution.mp4) Webinar Recording
  * [GHG Webinar](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/GHG%20Webinar.pdf) Slides
  * [High GWP Workshop](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/High%20GWP%20Workshop.pdf) Slides
  * [SERVM and Net CONE](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/SERVM%20and%20Net%20CONE%20v3.pdf) Slides
  * [T&D Workshop](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/T&D%20Workshop.pdf) Slides
  * [Post-Webinar Questions and Responses](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/CostEffectiveness/Answers%20to%20post%20Webinar%20questions.docx)


  * [Resolution E-5077](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M342/K084/342084398.PDF%20) in accordance with [D.20-04-1010](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M334/K734/334734544.PDF) in the R.14.10-003 proceeding.


**2019 ACC Update**
  * The 2019 updates to the ACC were adopted in [Resolution E-5014](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M310/K136/310136776.PDF) in accordance with D.19-15-019 in the R.14-10-003 proceeding.


**2018 ACC Update**
  * The 2018 updates to the ACC were adopted in [Resolution E-4942](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M218/K010/218010890.PDF) in accordance with D.16-06-007 in the R.14-10-003 proceeding.


**2017 ACC Update**
  * Per [D.17-08-022](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M195/K123/195123475.PDF%20), the only update to the ACC in 2017 was the interim greenhouse gas adder.


**2016 ACC Update**
  * The 2016 updates to the ACC were adopted in [Resolution E-4801](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M167/K779/167779209.PDF) in accordance with D.16-06-007 in the R.14-10-003 proceeding.


**2005-2015**
  * The avoided cost model was adopted in 2005 in [D.05-04-024](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/45284.PDF) in the [R.04-04-025](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R0404025%20) proceeding and was originally used to measure Energy Efficiency (EE) cost-effectiveness. The assumptions used in the ACC methodology required periodic updates to stay current with market conditions, prices, and trends. Thus, semi-regular improvements to the ACC modeling and data inputs were adopted in [D.06-06-063](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/57756.PDF), [D.09-09-047](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/PUBLISHED/GRAPHICS/107829.PDF), and [D.12-05-015](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/166830.PDF).

#  Energy Efficiency Workforce Education Training and Workforce Standards
The WE&T Program vision, set forth by the California Long Term Energy Efficiency Strategic Plan (CLTEESP), is to provide the human capital necessary to achieve California's economic energy efficiency and demand-side management potential. The WE&T program portfolio includes three sub programs: Career Connections, Career & Workforce Readiness (CWR), and Integrated Energy Education & Training (IEET).
You can find specific program information on the current WE&T portfolio [here](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/workforce-education-training/wetprograms2025.pdf).
You can find information on D.18-10-008 and its requirement of workforce standards [here](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/workforce-education-training/workforce_standards_2025.pdf).
You can find recent evaluations and studies related to WE&T and Workforce Standards [here](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/workforce-education-training/wetworkforceevaluations2025.pdf).
**Other Resources** :
[CLTEESP](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5305-eestrategicplan.pdf) - Original version of the Strategic Plan adopted in [D.0-09-040](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/91068.PDF)

  6. [Ex Ante Review](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/ex-ante-review)


#  Ex Ante Review
The Ex Ante process estimates the potential energy savings for an energy efficient measure before it is installed based on predictions of typical operating conditions and baseline usage. The process is the basis for utilities to claim savings for their energy efficiency portfolios. The Ex Ante process covers deemed measures (DEER and non-DEER), measures that have well-known and consistent performance characteristics, and custom measures.
The Database of Energy Efficient Resources and Ex Ante Regulatory Support [RFP No. 12 PS 5125 ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5326-exantescopeofwork.pdf "RFP No. 12 PS 5125 ")contains details on the scope of work for the four work areas of Ex Ante Review.
[Rulemaking R.13-11-005](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:15906284397852::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R1311005 "Rulemaking R.13-11-005") - Establishes a proceeding in which to fund the current energy efficiency portfolios through 2015, implement energy efficiency "rolling portfolios", and address various related policy; [Phase I Decision](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M129/K228/129228024.pdf "Phase I Decision") - October 27, 2014; [Compliance Filing Corrections from Decision](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/r/5327-requiredcorrectionsperd1410046orderparagraph16-20141103corrected.pdf "Compliance Filing Corrections from Decision") - November 11, 2014
## Ex Ante Review Fact Sheets
The set of fact sheets provide high level information and policy background to the Ex Ante review process. The purpose is to provide the public and stakeholders a better understanding of how the PUC conducts its review process.
[Ex Ante Review Fact Sheet #1](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5328-exantereviewfactsheet1costeffectiveness.pdf "Ex Ante Review Fact Sheet #1") provides information on how cost effectiveness is estimated.
[Ex Ante Review Fact Sheet #2 ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5329-exantereviewfactsheet2exanteprocess.pdf "Ex Ante Review Fact Sheet #2 ")provides information on different components of the review process and how each of them works.
[Ex Ante Review Fact Sheet #3](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5330-exantereviewfactsheet3hvac.pdf "Ex Ante Review Fact Sheet #3") provides information on high-level assumption for HVAC savings estimates currently used for the review process.
## Energy Modeling Tools Workshop Materials
On September 18, 2015, from 9:30am to 4:30pm, Commission staff is holding a workshop to exchange information on the predominant whole building energy modeling simulation tools in the market to understand what’s out there, how it’s used, and how we understand the strengths and weaknesses of each tool.
[Agenda](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/m/5331-modelingtoolsworkshop-agenda-9182015.docx "Agenda")
  * [Overview of Modeling Tools and their uses](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/o/5332-overviewofmodelingtools.pptx "Overview of Modeling Tools and their uses") (Pete Jacobs, Building Metrics)
  * [Energy Simulation Tools Assessment](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5333-energysimulationtoolsassessment.pptx "Energy Simulation Tools Assessment") (Holly Farrah, Cadmus)
  * [DOE-2 Overview](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/d/5334-doe2overview.pptx "DOE-2 Overview") (Joe Huang, White Box Technologies)
  * [EnergyPlus Overview](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5335-energyplusoverview.pptx "EnergyPlus Overview") (Larry Brackney, NREL)
