  * [Research on Modeling Tools ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/r/5337-researchonmodelingtools.pptx "Research on Modeling Tools ")(Phil Haves, LBL)
  * [Reflections from Building Modeling Experts](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/r/5338-reflectionsfromexpertshuang.pptx "Reflections from Building Modeling Experts") (Joe Huang, White Box Technologies)
  * [Ex Ante Development Issues](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5339-exantevaluedevelopmentissues-20150918.pptx "Ex Ante Development Issues") (Jeff Hirsch, JJH & Associates)


## DEER (Database for Energy Efficient Resources)
DEER, the Database for Energy Efficient Resources, provides energy savings estimates for typical energy efficient technologies and measures. The database contains information and data, such as unit energy savings, effective useful life values, and net to gross ratios for measures that are commonly installed in the marketplace. DEER is currently managed by the Energy Division, with funding provided by California ratepayers. The DEER website, [www.deeresources.com](http://www.deeresources.com/ "www.deeresources.com"), contains the database and all supporting documentation. Data can be accessed and downloaded through the database interface Remote Ex Ante Database Interface (READI) on the DEER website.
Instructions for analyzing measure savings impacts and parameters can be found [in this document](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/i/5340-instructionsonextractingmeasuregroupsandsavingsfromreaditool.pdf "in this document").
The [DEER Master Documentation](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/m/6072-master-documentation-set.zip "DEER Master Documentation") compiles DEER2008, DEER2011, and DEER2014 update documents and data into one directory. The Excel spreadsheet contains information and links to the individual documents. Please follow these steps to ensure the links work: (1) Extract the main folder onto your desktop and (2) Enable editing and macros in the excel file.
The [DEER Update Plan](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/5341-energydivisiondeerupdateplan.pdf "DEER Update Plan") outlines the process for updates for the 2014 and beyond cycles.
### Background
[D.05-01-055](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/67524.PDF "D.05-01-055") directed the Energy Division to provide oversight of research and analysis of the energy efficiency values. The Commission placed the updates to DEER under the management of regulatory staff.
In [D.05-04-051](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/45783.PDF "D.05-04-051"), the Commission determined that DEER shall be the source of assumptions used to estimate load impacts to the extent possible. 
[D.12-05-015](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M034/K299/34299795.PDF "D.12-05-015") adopted the DEER2011 release for use in planning the 2013-14 energy efficiency program cycle. In [R.09-11-014](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:2117084071791001::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R0911014 "R.09-11-014"), the Commission also determined that the codes and standards that will become effective in 2014 should be used to update the baseline values during the 2013-14 energy efficiency program cycle. 
### Upcoming and Current Events
**DEER Updates**
  * [Information on DEER updates](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/database-of-energy-efficiency-resources-updates). 


For more information, contact Peter Biermayer at peter.biermayer@cpuc.ca.gov.
## Non-DEER Workpaper Values for Energy Efficiency Measures
Program administrators and implementers develop non-DEER workpapers for measures that are not captured in DEER. Potential energy savings estimates are calculated based on best available information and extrapolation from DEER values or methods, as appropriate. Program administrators submit workpapers to the Energy Division for review on specific non-DEER measures. The Energy Division reviews a sub-selection of the submissions and releases dispositions with adjusted values, if needed. 
## Workpaper Development Training and Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
  * [Workpaper Development Training](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/w/6442459833-workpaper-development-training.pptx "Workpaper Development Training")
  * [FAQs](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/3/6442459608-3p-workpaper-faqs-v-1-13.pdf "FAQs")


### Background
Development, review and approval of non-DEER workpapers has evolved through several decisions. [D.09-09-047](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/PUBLISHED/GRAPHICS/107829.PDF "D.09-09-047") gave Energy Division authority to review and approve non-DEER workpapers and required Energy Division to develop a process for submittal, review and freezing of non-DEER measures. During the 2010-2012 program cycle, an administrative law judge ruling dated 11/18/2009 in [A.08-07-021](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:1235920668694201::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:A0807021 "A.08-07-021") provided a standardized review and approval process for Phase 2 non-DEER workpapers including:
  * Requirements for utilizing DEER values and methods in the development of non-DEER measures.
  * A timeline for workpaper review that required Commission staff to perform a preliminary review for additional information within 15 days and the final review within 25 days of receiving the additional review.
  * A requirement for consideration of the latest evaluation, measurement and verification published studies in the development of ex ante values including energy impacts, cost data, effective useful life, remaining useful life, and net-to-gross ratios.
  * Provision for retrospective staff reviews of frozen and workpapers not reviewed if the measures covered by a workpaper eventually become high-impact measures, measures with the largest amount of energy savings claimed by investor-owned utilities.
  * [D.10-04-029](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/116710.PDF "D.10-04-029") approved [Application 08-07-021](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:1235920668694201::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:A0807021 "Application 08-07-021") and required investor-owned utilities to cooperate with energy division to allow upfront consultation regarding workpapers. [D.11-07-030](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/139858.PDF "D.11-07-030") approved all of [Application 08-07-021](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:1235920668694201::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:A0807021 "Application 08-07-021") in regards to Phase 2 review, except the provision for retrospective staff review, which was then removed.


[D.12-05-015](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/166830.PDF "D.12-05-015") is the guidance decision covering 2013-2014 energy efficiency applications and includes a process for workpaper review that builds upon the process established in the previous decisions. [D.12-05-015](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/166830.PDF "D.12-05-015") included the following requirements:
  * Ex ante values shall be updated in consideration of 2006-2008 evaluation results.
  * Utilities shall include workpapers in their initial 2013-2014 applications for all measures that are not specifically included in the DEER database. (NOTE: Staff and utilities refer to workpapers submitted with the initial 2013-2014 applications as “Phase 1” workpapers.)
  * Update and review of workpapers shall be given to High Impact Measures.
  * If Staff is unable to review all workpapers submitted with the initial 2013-2014 workpapers, the un-reviewed workpapers shall receive “interim approval,” and Staff may review any of these in the future, with revisions applied prospectively.
  * A mid-cycle review is included that prescribes a process for reviewing any workpapers that have interim approval as well as any Phase 2 workpapers that are submitted during the cycle.
  * New non-DEER measures may be added during the program cycle. Staff may assign new non-DEER measures “interim approval” prior to the completion of any necessary research.
  * A Phase 2 submission and review process is included that limits the time for staff review of workpapers and does not allow the use of the disposition of “conditional approval” that was previously allowed under [D.11-07-030](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/139858.PDF "D.11-07-030").
  * Every six months Commission Staff will develop a draft resolution covering any disputed workpapers that will be subject to a Commission vote.


## Custom Measures and Projects
Custom measures and projects are site-specific energy efficiency projects. Custom projects require unique calculations for each project, as they do not rely on fixed DEER or workpaper values. For custom measures and projects the ex ante values cannot be forecasted in advance since the preliminary ex ante values are not known until the project is identified. Final ex ante values are not developed until the project is completed. Commission Decision [11-07-030](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/139858.PDF "11-07-030") establishes a clear process by which ex ante energy savings estimates from custom measures and projects are reviewed.
### Background
[D.11-07-030](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/139858.PDF "D.11-07-030") approves that the Energy Division custom project ex ante review process is reasonable and should be adopted. [Attachment B](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/139860.PDF "Attachment B") of the filing details how the investor-owned utilities’ energy savings claims will be reviewed:
  * Under the General Review process, the Energy Division will review tools, measures, and projects, as well as inputs to the tools for selected projects. The tool reviews will be done on a prospective basis.
  * The Pre-Installation Review process allows the Energy Division to perform a parallel review process with the investor-owned utilities, and then allows for the Energy Division to provide inputs and recommendations.
  * The Post-Installation Review process is used to provide the Energy Division with opportunity to review the accuracy of energy savings claims assumed by the investor-owned utility prior to the utility making its final incentive payment to its customer.
  * Under the Investor-Owned Utility Claim Review process, the Energy Division conducts a review of energy savings for custom projects to ensure that recommendations made by Energy Division were accurately reflected in the claim.


Customer process guidance documents [can be found on this page](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents).

#  Energy Efficiency Procurement and Solicitations
### **Energy Efficiency Portfolios and Business Plan:**
  * [Rulemaking R.13-11-005](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:15906284397852::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R1311005 "Rulemaking R.13-11-005") - The proceeding through which the CPUC funds and sets current energy efficiency portfolios, energy efficiency goals, and addresses various related policy goals. [November 14th, 2013] 
    * [Proceeding A.22-02-005](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:A2202005) – The proceeding detailing the Approval for the 2024-2031 Energy Efficiency Business Plan and the 2024-2027 Portfolio Plan; any decision made under this proceeding now falls under R.13-11-005. [February 15th, 2022] 
    * [D.23-06-055](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M512/K907/512907396.PDF): Approves the new budgets for the 2024-2027 energy efficiency portfolios and 2024-2031 business plans for nine portfolio administrators, creates RuralREN, adopts equity and market support segments, with ways to identify metrics and goals to be measured, for the energy efficiency portfolios, and promotes the market access approach. Includes modifications to the “**hard-to-reach customers** ” definition provided in D.18-05-041 and “small business size” established in Resolution E-4939. [July 3rd, 2023] 
    * [D.23-08-005](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M517/K027/517027629.PDF): Adopts the total system benefit and energy savings goals for ratepayer-funded energy efficiency portfolios for 2024-2035. [August 16th, 2023]
    * [D.18-05-041](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M215/K706/215706139.PDF) – Provides a refined definition of “**disadvantaged communities** ” and “**hard-to-reach customers**.” [June 5th, 2018]
    * [Resolution E-4939](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M232/K460/232460214.PDF) – Provides the definition and scope of a “**small business** ” and customers to it. [October 11th, 2018]


  * [Rulemaking R.06-04-010](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R0604010) - Order Instituting Rulemaking to examine the Commission's post-2005 energy efficiency policies, programs, evaluation, measurement, and verification, and related issues. [April 13th, 2006] 
    * [D.10-04-029](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/116710.PDF) – This decision, among other items, sets out the roles and relationships among the CPUC’s Energy Division, California’s investor-owned utilities (IOU), and stakeholders regarding Evaluation, Measurement and Verification (EM&V) of energy efficiency programs and directed that the review of completed IOU workpapers regarding ex-ante savings estimates are subject to Energy Division review and approval, as set forth in an Administrative Law Judge Ruling of [November 18, 2009](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/EFILE/RULINGS/110002.PDF) in Application 08-07-021, et al. Each IOU shall cooperate with Energy Division to allow upfront consultation regarding such workpapers. [April 21st, 2010] 
    * [D.11-07-030](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/139858.PDF) - This decision adopts, among other items, an Energy Division custom project ex ante value review process (see [Attachment B](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/139860.PDF)). [July 22nd, 2011]


### **Cost-Effectiveness:**
  * [Rulemaking R.22-11-013](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R2211013) – The proceeding that covers Distributed Energy Resource (DER) program cost-effectiveness issues, data access and use, and equipment performance standards. All of the latest updates and decisions related to the **Avoided Cost Calculator (ACC)** fall under this proceeding. [November 17th, 2022] 
    * [D.19-05-019](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M293/K833/293833387.PDF) – Adopts new cost-effectiveness analysis tests for DER programs. Utilizes the **Total Resource Cost** (TRC) test as the primary test of cost-effectiveness, while also considering the results of the Program Administrator Cost (PAC) and Ratepayer Impact Measure (RIM) cost-effectiveness tests. Lastly, begins testing the Societal Cost Test (SCT) to see if it will help meet California’s carbon reduction objectives. [May 21st, 2019] 
    * [D.22-05-002](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M474/K624/474624547.PDF) – Covers the need for continued updates of the Avoided Cost Calculator and the need to align the ACC with the Integrated Resource Planning proceeding. Thus, the ACC will rely upon an adopted Reference System Plan (RSP) or a Preferred System Plan (PSP). [May 6th, 2022] 


### **Clean Energy Financing Process:**
  * [Rulemaking R.20-08-022](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R2008022) – The proceeding to investigate and design clean energy financing options for electricity and natural gas customers. [August 27th, 2020] 
    * [D.23-08-026](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M517/K717/517717993.PDF) – Authorizes the expansion of **on-bill financing** programs administered by the IOUs to support clean energy technologies beyond energy efficiency. [August 18th, 2023] 


### **Energy Efficiency Processes and Systems:**
  * [D.21-05-031](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M385/K864/385864616.PDF) – Addresses policy issues on the identification of energy efficiency potential and the setting of goals for program administrators to achieve. Adopts the Total System Benefit metric, segments energy efficiency program portfolios into resource acquisition, market support, and equity, and makes changes to the rolling portfolio framework and regulatory process. [May 26th, 2021]
    * [Program Closure Checklist](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-proceeding-activities/program-closure-checklist-required-by-d2105031-12312021.docx) - As required by D.21-05-031 Ordering Paragraph 12, Energy Division created a checklist of steps required by portfolio administers and Energy Division staff to close an existing or launch a new energy efficiency program.
  * [D.23-02-002](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M501/K931/501931085.PDF) – Adopts improvements to the third-party solicitation process for the Commission’s energy efficiency portfolio. [February 3rd, 2023] 


### **Fuel Substitution and Natural Gas:**
  * [Rulemaking R.17-02-002](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56::::RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:I1702002) – Proceeding on the feasibility of minimizing or eliminating the use of Aliso Canyon Natural Gas Storage Facility while maintaining energy and electric reliability for the nearby region. [February 9th, 2017] 
  * [D.19-08-009](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M310/K159/310159146.PDF) – Sets up the Fuel Substitution Test to address greenhouse gas emission goals and declares that fuel substitution will be reflected in the cost-effectiveness evaluation of a program administrator’s energy efficiency portfolio. [August 5th, 2019]
  * [D.23-04-035](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M505/K808/505808197.PDF) – Sets forth a new framework and criteria for the purpose of reducing ratepayer funded incentives for natural gas energy efficiency measures that have a Viable Electric Alternative (VEA). [April 14th, 2023] 
    * [Staff Proposal](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Efile/G000/M496/K397/496397066.PDF) referenced for this decision.


### **Deemed Ex Ante Measures and Measure Packages:**
The log in site for Up-to-date information on the Deemed Ex Ante measures can be found at [Home - CEDARS (sound-data.com)](https://cedars.sound-data.com/).
Information on Deemed Measure Packages can be found at: [DEER Resources - CEDARS (sound-data.com)](https://cedars.sound-data.com/deer-resources/)
Under “**Deemed Measure Plans** ”
  * All the most recent DEER Update Resolutions that show measure package parameter updates by year.
  * Measure package dispositions.
  * Guidance documents – clarify and interpret Decision and Resolution language.


Under “**Tools** ”
  * How to incorporate approved building simulation software into measure packages.
  * Water heater calculator.


Under**“DEER Database”**
  * Archived past dispositions.


### **Custom Project Review (CPR):**
CPUC [D.09-09-047](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/PUBLISHED/FINAL_DECISION/107829.htm), [D.11-07-030](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/PUBLISHED/FINAL_DECISION/139858.htm), and the [CPR Guidance Page](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents)
  * **Protect ratepayer funds** by ensuring reliable savings estimates. 
  * Customer incentives and program implementer performance payments are based on the estimated efficiency savings.
  * **Catch errors** that may have escaped the program administrator’s (PA) internal engineering review process to improve the reliability of the efficiency savings estimates. Review items such as:
    * Efficiency savings calculations methodology
    * Input parameters assumption
    * Savings verification measurement methodologies and data collection
  * **Help Program Administrators improve their internal review process**.
  * **Ensure project compliance** with CPUC policy and program rules.


### **Strategic Energy Management (SEM):**
  * SEM was launched in 2018 as an engagement program targeting the industrial & agriculture market sectors ([D.16-08-019](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M166/K232/166232537.docx)) 
    * Net-to-Gross Ratio of 1 
    * Existing Conditions Baseline
    * 5-year Effective Useful Life (EUL)
  * SEM is a holistic, long-term customer engagement using a whole-facility program approach. 
  * In SEM, regression models and existing conditions baselines are used to estimate savings from all program activities at the facility.
  * SEM includes capital projects, behavioral, retro-commissioning, and operational (BRO), and integrated demand side management measures.


SEM will be expanded to non-industrial non-residential sectors in the near future pending the completion of an expansion study as directed by [D.23-02-002](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M501/K931/501931085.docx).

#  Energy Efficiency Reporting
**Report Types**
  * Annual report narratives and spreadsheets
  * Cap and Targets reports
  * Quarterly business plan metrics reports
  * Quarterly LGP Strategic Plan reports
  * AB 841 School EE reports
  * Low Income EE reports

#  Energy Efficiency Market Transformation
In pursuit of California's ambitious [Climate Action Plan](https://www.gov.ca.gov/2022/11/16/california-releases-worlds-first-plan-to-achieve-net-zero-carbon-pollution/) to reduce our state's greenhouse gas emissions to net zero by 2045, energy efficiency is one of our key solutions. In addition to the main EE portfolio, the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) created a Market Transformation Administrator (CalMTA) to make energy efficient technologies and practices more available in the market. The objective of this program is to actively transform selected markets in support of achieve California's climate goals. The framework for the creation of CalMTA can be found in [D.19-12-021](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?DocFormat=ALL&DocID=321507615).
### Important Documents:
  * [Market Transformation Policy Manual](https://calmta.org/resources-and-reports/calmta-releases-market-transformation-policy-manual/)– CPUC Policies and guidelines applicable to Market Transformation and CalMTA 
  * [Evaluation Framework](https://calmta.org/evaluating-market-transformation-progress/) – Describes the policies, principles, and high-level approaches that CalMTA will use to verify energy savings and effectiveness ofits portfolio of MT initiatives 
  * [2025 Annual Operations Plan](https://calmta.org/resources-and-reports/2025-operations-plan/) – Presents work planned for CalMTA’s program year 2025 needed to reach the goals of the program 
  * [CalMTA Q3 2024 Activity Report](https://calmta.org/resources-and-reports/3rd-quarter-2024-activity-report/) – Report summarizing recent milestones in developing a portfolio of market transformation initiatives (MTIs) 


Application & MTI Plans
  * [Application](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?DocFormat=ALL&DocID=550500737) for Approval of Initial Tranche of Statewide Energy Efficiency Market Transformation Initiatives 
  * [Induction Cooking MTI Plan](https://calmta.org/resources-and-reports/induction-cooking-mti-plan/) – seeks to accelerate market adoption of efficient induction cooktops and ranges to provide a high-quality cooking experience with the added health and safety benefits afforded by induction technology. 
  * [Room Heat Pumps MTI Plan](https://calmta.org/resources-and-reports/room-heat-pumps-mti-plan/) – aims to accelerate market adoption of room heat pumps to provide efficient heating and cooling in existing small, single-family, and multifamily households.


### Other Resouces:
  * For information on the purpose and membership of the Market Transformation Advisory Board (MTAB), visit the[About the MTAB](https://calmta.org/advisory-board/) and [Advisory Board FAQ](https://calmta.org/advisory-board-faq/) pages

#  Energy Efficiency Program Implementers

Listed below are the California investor-owned utilities and local government agencies authorized to implement CPUC-directed Energy Efficiency programs.

#  Energy Efficiency Potential and Goals Studies
In 2004 the Commission adopted aggressive ten-year energy savings goals to meets its commitment to make Energy Efficiency the resource of first choice, as established in the [Energy Action Plan](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PUBLISHED/REPORT/51604.htm "Energy Action Plan"). The goals serve several important roles in the State regulatory framework. 
  1. **To provide guidance for the utilities’ next energy efficiency portfolios:** The energy efficiency goals were first adopted in Decision [(D.)04-09-060](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/published/FINAL_DECISION/40212.htm "\(D.\)04-09-060") to set the benchmark that the IOU energy efficiency programs were expected to achieve. The goal-setting process set a framework for the program planning cycle, determining the targets for utility energy efficiency program portfolio performance. Based on this decision, the CPUC adopted aggressive annual and ten-year cumulative goals for verified electric and natural gas savings and allowed the utilities to develop their own programs and portfolios. To measure the IOUs' progress toward their goals, the Energy Division developed an extensive evaluation, measurement and verification program (EM&V) to verify that utility programs meet their commitments. Since D.04-09-060, the Commission adopted interim goals in 2007 in D.07-10-032, D.09-09-047, and D.12-05-015. 
  2. **To update the forecast for energy procurement planning:** As the Energy Action Plan established energy efficiency as first in the loading order, the state must adopt a long-term benchmark that can be used in utility energy procurement planning. The IOUs' energy efficiency goals adopted from this study will be incorporated into the California Energy Commission’s (CEC's) [ _Integrated Energy Policy Report_](http://www.energy.ca.gov/2009_energypolicy/index.html "Integrated Energy Policy Report"), which establishes the demand forecast for long-term procurement planning. This forecast is an input into the CPUC’s [Long Term Procurement Planning Proceeding (LTPP)](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/electric-power-procurement/long-term-procurement-planning/long-term-procurement-plan-background), which determines the generation resources that energy efficiency is expected to offset in order to minimize costs to ratepayers.* 
  3. **To inform strategic contributions to California’s greenhouse gas reduction targets:** The [California Global Warming Solutions Act of 2006 (AB 32)](http://www.arb.ca.gov/cc/ab32/ab32.htm "CaliforniaGlobal Warming Solutions Act of 2006 \(AB 32\)") relies on intensified energy efficiency efforts across California. The [California Air Resources Board's (CARB's) Scoping Plan](http://www.arb.ca.gov/cc/scopingplan/document/adopted_scoping_plan.pdf "California Air Resources Board's\(CARB's\) Scoping Plan") for Assembly Bill (AB) 32 establishes a statewide energy efficiency target of at least 32,000 GWh and 800 million therms by 2020, making it the second largest component in the State’s overall emissions reduction program. Future EE goals updates will have more implications for forecasts of GHG reductions to be provided by EE policy initiatives. 
  4. **To set benchmarks for shareholder incentives:** In [D.07-09-043](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PUBLISHED/FINAL_DECISION/73172.htm "D.07-09-043"), the Commission adopted shareholder incentives for the 2006-2008 energy efficiency program cycle to encourage the utilities to meet or surpass their energy efficiency goals. In [D.13-09-023](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M076/K775/76775903.PDF "D.13-09-023"), the Commission modified the shareholder incentive mechanism for the 2013-14 program cycle to again tie shareholder earning to energy savings accomplishments. 


## Goals Background and History
Since 2003, the CPUC has established energy efficiency goals based on legislative requirements set in [SB 1037](http://www.leginfo.ca.gov/pub/05-06/bill/sen/sb_1001-1050/sb_1037_bill_20050929_chaptered.html "SB 1037"). 
  * [History of Goals](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/h/4069-history-and-goals.pdf "History of Goals"): Provides background of Commission decisions that have established the structure and application of goals. 


## Potential & Goals Studies:
  * [2025](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-potential-and-goals-studies/2025-potential-and-goals-study)
  * [2023](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-potential-and-goals-studies/2023-potential-and-goals-study)
  * [2021](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-potential-and-goals-studies/2021-potential-and-goals-study)
  * [2019](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-potential-and-goals-studies/2019-potential-and-goals-study)
  * [2018](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-potential-and-goals-studies/2018-potential-and-goals-study)
  * [2015](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-potential-and-goals-studies/2015-potential-and-goals-study)

#  Self-Generation Incentive Program (SGIP)
The CPUC's Self-Generation Incentive Program (SGIP) provides incentives to support existing, new, and emerging distributed energy resources. SGIP provides incentives for qualifying distributed energy systems installed on the customer's side of the utility meter. Qualifying technologies include wind turbines, waste heat to power technologies, pressure reduction turbines, internal combustion engines, microturbines, gas turbines, fuel cells, linear generators, advanced energy storage systems, and combined solar and energy storage systems. 
**The Residential Solar and Storage Equity Incentives Are Available for Reservation Beginning June 2, 2025. Available to any low-income residential electric and/or gas customer in California. LADWP will be opening applications before the end of 2025.**
**The CPUC’s Self-Generation Incentive Program (SGIP) offers incentives for installing paired solar and energy storage technology at low-income residential properties.** These paired systems can function during a power outage, provide bill savings, and provide benefits to the grid.
The CPUC has authorized funding of $280 million for the Residential Solar and Storage Equity budget in SGIP. The best way to get started in accessing SGIP is to reach out to an installer who can help navigate the application process. Use the [Approved SGIP Developer List ](https://www.selfgenca.com/documents/developer/approved)to help find an installer in your area. (Please note that the CPUC does not endorse or recommend any of these installers.) 
For the most up-to-date information on the current status of the SGIP budget and whether or not a particular budget is open or closed, please visit the SGIP home page by [clicking here](https://www.selfgenca.com/home/program_metrics/). To view the latest edition of the Self-Generation Incentive Program Handbook, [click here](https://www.selfgenca.com/home/resources/).
For information on how to apply for incentives in your area, please contact the Program Administrator for your utility found at the bottom of this page.
## Recent News and Quick Links
  * [Proceeding R.20-05-012](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:56:0::NO:RP,57,RIR:P5_PROCEEDING_SELECT:R2005012) for all Decisions, Rulings, and Party Comments on SGIP since May 2020. 
  * The SGIP Program Administrators (PAs) host a quarterly Public Workshop. Notices and past presentations can be found [here](https://www.selfgenca.com/home/about/). 
  * SGIP conducts regular reports to monitor and evaluate the impact of the program and the administrative processes of the Program Administrators. The full list of reports can be found [here](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/self-generation-incentive-program/self-generation-incentive-program-evaluation-reports). 
  * The SGIP Weekly Projects report is available [here](https://www.selfgenca.com/documents/reports/statewide_projects "here")
  * [Decision 24-03-071](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?DocFormat=ALL&DocID=527963349) establishing the Residential Solar and Storage Equity budget pursuant to Assembly Bill 209 and implements other program changes. 
      * February 20, 2025: [Resolution E-5373](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M557/K930/557930117.PDF) finalizes the implementation of the IRA tax credit in SGIP.
      * January 30, 2025: [Resolution E-5362](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M556/K402/556402829.PDF) finalizes the opening of the new Residential Solar and Storage Equity budget.
      * December 19, 2025: [Resolution E-5360](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M551/K507/551507127.PDF) finalizes the new residential storage sizing rules in SGIP. 
  * April 11, 2022: [Decision 22-04-036](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M467/K581/467581288.PDF) establishing the SGIP Heat Pump Water Heater Program through 2025. 
  * January 27, 2020: [Decision 20-01-021](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M325/K979/325979689.PDF "Decision 20-01-021") authorizing ratepayer collections of $166 million annually for the years 2020 to 2024 to fund the SGIP and implement program revision pursuant to Senate Bill 700 and other program changes.
  * September 18, 2019: [Decision 19-09-027](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/SearchRes.aspx?DocFormat=ALL&DocID=313975481 "Decision 19-09-027") establishing the SGIP Equity Resiliency budget and approving $10 million to support the San Joaquin Valley disadvantaged community pilot projects.
  * SGIP proceeding R.12-11-005 includes links to earlier Rulings, Comments and Decisions.


Program Modification 
Applicants who would like to propose new technologies or specific program modifications for consideration by the SGIP Program Administrators and the CPUC must complete the Request Form contained in the Program Modification Guideline, [here](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/p/7721-prog-modific-guide-11-15-031-1-.pdf "SGIP Program modification guide"). Applicants must follow the CPUC's [Rules of Practice and Procedure](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/rules-of-practice-and-procedure) for any filings.
## Helpful Resources
  * [SGIP Fact Sheet - Residential Solar and Storage Equity](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/self-generation-incentive-program/sgip-fact-sheet---residential-solar-and-storage-equity_ver2.pdf)


## Eligibility for Program
Customers must meet various criteria in order to be eligible for SGIP rebates. **Please check the[SGIP Handbook](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/self-generation-incentive-program/2025-sgip-handbook-v1.pdf) for the information about additional ****eligibility criteria and performance requirements. All applicants have one year after reserving funds to meet the program requirements which include customer enrollment in a qualified Demand Response program. Contact your Program Administrator with questions**. 
SGIP Budget |  Incentive Rate ($/kWh) |  Customer Sector |  Additional Eligibility Criteria?  
---|---|---|---  
Residential Solar and Storage Equity |  Storage: 1,100 Solar: 3,100/kW |  Residential Customer |  Yes  
San Joaquin Valley Residential (available through 2025) |  1,100 |  PG&E or SCE residential customer |  Yes  
Equity Resiliency (available through 2025) |  1,000 |  IOU* residential or non-residential customer |  Yes  
Small Residential Storage (available through 2025) |  150 |  IOU residential customer |   
San Joaquin Valley Non-Residential (available through 2025) |  1,000 |  SCE non-residential customer |  Yes  
Non-Residential Equity (available through 2025) |  850 |  IOU non-residential customer |  Yes  
Large-Scale Storage (available through 2025) |  250 |  IOU non-residential customer |   
Generation (available through 2025) |  2,000/kW |  IOU non-residential customer |   
*IOU customer refers to a customer of PG&E, Southern California Edison, SoCalGas, or SDG&E. Unbundled IOU customers are also customers of CCA.
## Learn More
Local Program Administrators are the primary contact for information on SGIP in your area. We encourage you to reach out to them with any questions. Your Program Administrator depends on who your utility is. For customers of other utilities, including publicly owned utilities and co-ops, interested in the Residential Solar and Storage Equity budget, refer to [this list](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M528/K053/528053537.PDF) for the Program Administrator for your application.
  * Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP)
    * Email: sgip@ladwp.com


  * Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E)
    * Website: [www.pge.com/sgip](http://www.pge.com/sgip "www.pge.com/sgip")
    * Email: selfgen@pge.com


  * San Diego Gas & Electric (SDG&E) – Via Center for Sustainable Energy (CSE)
    * Website: [www.energycenter.org/self-generation-incentive-program](http://www.energycenter.org/self-generation-incentive-program "www.energycenter.org/self-generation-incentive-program")
    * Email: sgip@energycenter.org


  * Southern California Edison (SCE)
    * Website: [www.sce.com/SGIP](http://www.sce.com/SGIP "www.sce.com/SGIP")
    * Email: SGIPGroup@sce.com


  * Southern California Gas Company (SoCalGas) 
    * Website: [www.socalgas.com/for-your-business/power-generation/self-generation-incentive](http://www.socalgas.com/for-your-business/power-generation/self-generation-incentive "www.socalgas.com/for-your-business/power-generation/self-generation-incentive")
    * Email: selfgeneration@socalgas.com

#  IDSM
### Current 2023 IDSM: 
In the [2023 EE Portfolio Business Plan](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M512/K907/512907396.PDF), it was discussed that historically, IDSM had focused on integration of EE and DR approaches (in D.18-05-041), but moving forward, could include fuel substitution, self-generation, and storage as well. It is stated in this decision that IDSM funding should not be used on event-based demand response approaches and that the goal is to promote ongoing load shifting that reduced peak consumption. We are at a time where we need to transform the way customers understand, use, and manage their energy use through an integrated approach.
IDSM Through the Years:
2018-2023: In the [2018 EE Rolling Portfolio Business Plan](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M215/K706/215706139.PDF), Commission staff proposed to repurpose the Integrated Demand Side Management (IDSM) budget to fund limited integration aspects of Energy Efficiency (EE) and Demand Response (DR) portfolios. The goal was to gain additional demand response value for little incremental cost since IDSM funds are primarily used for DR when EE investments are already being made.
Pre-2018: Beginning in 2007 with [D.07-10-032](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/WORD_PDF/FINAL_DECISION/74107.PDF), the Commission required the utilities to integrate their customer demand side programs, such as energy efficiency, self-generation, advanced metering, and demand response coherently and efficiently. This integration evolved over several years, with new milestones and outputs for IDSM programs beingintroduced in [D.09-09-047](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/PUBLISHED/GRAPHICS/107829.PDF) for future program cycles. 
### Other Resources:
  * [IDSM Guidance for Tier 3 Advice Letters](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/idsm-guidance-for-tier-3-als.pdf)


##  RECENT NEWS AND MEETINGS 
  * IDSM programs will need to be proposed by PAs through the submission of Tier 3 advice letters no later than March 15, 2024, for programs to be launched during the portfolio period (2024-2027)

#  Regulatory Services
We regulate "investor-owned" or privately owned electric, natural gas, telecommunications, railroad, rail crossings, rail transit, passenger carriers — limousines, charter buses, ferries, transportation network companies, such as Uber or Lyft — and water utilities.
## [ Audits ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/utility-audits-risk-and-compliance-division/utility-audits-branch)
Auditing, accounting, financial, and advisory services on regulated utilities and law and Commission directive compliance monitoring. 
## [ Enforcement and Citations ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/regulatory-services/enforcement-and-citations)
Ensuring that the CPUC’s statutory mandates are carried out and that ratepayers and the public are protected from safety, reliability, service quality, and other violations. 
## [ Licensing ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/regulatory-services/licensing)
Licensing and registration of transportation, communications, and energy service providers. 
## [ Rail ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/rail-safety-division)
We regulate rail safety including freight, inter-city and commuter railroads, rail transit and rail crossings. 
## [ Safety ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/regulatory-services/safety)
Oversight, monitoring, and audits to ensure the safety and reliability of regulated consumer services. 
##  How can we help?
Emergency? **Call 911**

#  Strategic Energy Management (SEM)
SEM was launched in 2018 ([D.16-08-019](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M166/K232/166232537.PDF)). SEM involves holistic, long-term customer engagement**,** and creates multi-year, phased
[expanded](https://pda.energydataweb.com/#!/documents/3978/view) to non-industrial market sectors including commercial, agriculture, and multi-family, if the current SEM guidebooks are followed. The[D.23-02-002),](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M501/K931/501931085.PDF) supports this expansion, while allowing application of the program savings parameters listed below.
In SEM, regression models and existing condition baselines are used to estimate savings from all program activities at a customer facility. SEM activities include capital projects, behavioral, retro-commissioning, operational (BRO) measures, and promotes customer knowledge of integrating demand side management (IDSM) measures.
If the prescribed program design and guidebooks are followed, eligible project savings will be based on: 
  * Net-to-Gross Ratio of 1 
  * Existing Conditions Baseline
  * 5-year Effective Useful Life (EUL)


### How SEM Works, visually:
![SEM - HOW IT WORKS](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/images/sem.png?h=617.222&w=557.444&hash=DC8D04C4E3C8538C89CD0C0CF746E4DB)
IOU/PA Links:
PGE: [Energy-Saving Programs (pge.com)](https://www.pge.com/en/save-energy-and-money/energy-saving-programs.html)
SCE: [Third-Party Energy Efficiency Programs (sce.com)](https://www.sce.com/business/savings-incentives/third-party-energy-efficiency-programs)
SDG&E: [Main Business Savings Center Landing page | San Diego Gas & Electric (sdge.com)](https://www.sdge.com/business/save-energy-and-money)
SoCalGas: [Energy Savings | SoCalGas](https://www.socalgas.com/savings)
MCE: [MCE's AIR and Strategic Energy Management Programs (mcecleanenergy.org)](https://www.mcecleanenergy.org/energy-management/)
### Guidance Documents:
[California SEM Program M&V Guide](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/sem/ca-sem-mv-guide-v40-optimized.pdf)
[California SEM Design Guide For: Cycle 1, 2, and 3](https://pda.energydataweb.com/api/view/2647/CA_3_CYCLE_SEM_Design_Guide_V1.01.pdf)
[California SEM Design Guide: Core SEM and Graduate Pathway](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/sem/ca-sem-design-guide-v21-optimized.pdf)
[Implementation Plan (IP) Template](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/sem/energy-efficiency-implementation-plan-template-ver-31-dec-2025.docx) (Dec 2025)
[IP Change Summary Form](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/sem/energy-efficiency-implementation-plan-change-summary-form-template-ver-31-dec-2025.docx) (Dec 2025)
[SEM Custom Project Guidance Memo](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/sem/ed-sem-capital-project-treatment-memo_20250324.docx)
[Project Feasibility Study (PFS) Form](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/sem/statewide-project-feasibility-study-template-may-2025.xlsx): SEM-Custom projects must submit a PFS for Custom Project Review (CPR)

April 24th, 2024 - Strategic Energy Management Report and Expansion Study

  * [SEM/CPUC Public Expansion Study Slide Deck](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/sem/sem-expansion-study-results-presentation.pdf)
  * [Strategic Energy Management Expansion Study](https://pda.energydataweb.com/api/view/3978/CPUC_SEM_Expansion_Study_Final%20Report.pdf)


April 30th, 2025 – Strategic Energy Management Impact Report
  * [Group D SEM PY2023 Evaluation Slide Deck](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/sem/py2023-sem-results-presentation_draft.pptx)

#  Market Access Program
# Background
In response to Governor Newsom’s [July 30, 2021 Emergency Proclamation](https://www.gov.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/07/Energy-Emergency-Proc-7-30-21.pdf), which directed state agencies to address a statewide shortage of electricity, the Commission authorized the Market Access Program as a strategy to reduce peak demand. This program was created by [D.21-12-011](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M429/K805/429805997.PDF), which authorized up to $150 million to fund projects that are incremental to the main energy efficiency portfolio. Market Access incentivizes peak savings (7 to 9pm) during the summers of 2022 and 2023, with payments based on actual savings at the meter. This page will contain information about the progress of the program.
  * Market Access Guidance
    * [D.21-12-011  
](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M429/K805/429805997.PDF)
  * Advice Letter Filings
    * [MCE AL 60-E](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/mce-advice-letter-60-e-map-proposal.pdf)
    * [PG&E AL 4572-G 6498-E](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/pge-al-4572-g_6498-e.pdf)
    * [SCE AL 4715-E](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/4715e-part-1-of-1.pdf)
    * [SDG&E AL 3951-E](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/sdge-al-3951-e.pdf)   

  * Implementation Plans 
    * [Marin Clean Energy](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/implementation-plan/mce-implementation_plan_v5.pdf)
    * [PG&E](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/implementation-plan/pge-implementation-plan_v1.pdf)
    * [SDG&E](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/implementation-plan/sdge-implementation_plan_v1.pdf)
    * [Southern California Edison](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/implementation-plan/sce-implementation_plan_v1.pdf)
  * Data
    * [Market Access Program Combined Reporting – September 2023](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/market-access-program/september-2023-combined-reporting-111323.xlsx)
      * Notes on reporting:
        * Measured savings and total system benefit (TSB) are reported quarterly and submitted to the Commission two months after each quarter
        * Marin Clean Energy has multiple programs that address peak demand, but only their Residential Efficiency FLEXmarket program is included in the reporting.
  *     * [Marin Clean Energy - Residential Efficiency FLEXmarket](https://www.demandflexmarket.com/mce-residential-efficiency-market.html)
    * [PG&E - Market Access Program](https://www.pge.com/en_US/large-business/save-energy-and-money/energy-management-programs/energy-incentives/market-access-program/market-access-program.page?WT.mc_id=Vanity_marketaccess)
    * [SDG&E - Summer Reliability Market Access Program](https://www.sdge.com/summer-reliability-market-access-program)
    * [Southern California Edison - Summer Reliability Program](https://www.sce.com/business/savings-incentives/summer-reliability-program)

#  Newsroom
Browse our latest news, learn about our meetings, sign up to receive documents, and more!
![Man sitting at kitchen table, smiling at his open laptop.](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/news-and-outreach/images/featurestories.jpg?mw=645&hash=479E7B672F2560D48F450FC3383B3C4E)
## [ Featured Stories ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/news-and-updates/all-news?search=&topicCategoryFilters=&programCategoryFilters=&newsTypeFilters=6ff1058f6aa24b49a3d82b49264452d6&divisionFilters=&sort=&activeFilters=newstypes%7C&page=1&industryFilters=&utilityFilters=&sectionFilters=&publicationFilters=)
Read our latest blogs, featured stories, and more! 
![male reporter taking notes and holding a microphone](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/news-and-outreach/images/prdatabase.jpg?mw=645&hash=DF751B0634EC11D224411C801B330EB3)
## [ Press Releases and Media Advisories ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/news-and-updates/all-news)
Read our latest press releases and media advisories. 
![pages of a calendar being flipped](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/news-and-outreach/images/meetingevents.jpg?mw=645&hash=5E63020F4F2185E6D9BA92D5DEC9BBB3)
## [ Meetings and Events Calendar ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/events-and-meetings)
Looking for more information on upcoming meetings or events that are happening at the CPUC? 
![computer keyboard with blue keys spelling out NEWS](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/news-and-outreach/images/latestnews.jpg?mw=645&hash=4E52639C78F617F03455E5662A15AE59)
## [ Press Release Database ](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/newssearchform.aspx)
CPUC press releases and media advisories, from July 2000 to the present. 
![A view of the speaker's table taken from the back of the room. There are many people in attendance.](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/news-and-outreach/images/pphmeeting_sd.jpg?mw=645&hash=88845E2F686FCEFF477D6D2CC9931997)
## [ Public Participation Hearings ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/cpuc-public-participation-hearings)
The CPUC often holds Public Participation Hearings (PPHs) and other events to hear from the public on issues before the CPUC. 
![Smiling woman typing at her laptop](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/news-and-outreach/images/subscription.jpg?mw=645&hash=6254AE03BCE100049AF132113BF9D8A4)
## [ Subscription Service ](http://subscribecpuc.cpuc.ca.gov/)
This free service provides the public the ability to subscribe to documents published on the CPUC's website that are associated with formal proceedings, resolutions, agenda related materials, and press releases. 
![A man's hand and laptop keyboard are visible. There are document icons floating in space before him.](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/news-and-outreach/images/newfilings.jpg?mw=645&hash=83E9E5A1222586910DDB38414D2158B7)
## [ New Filings ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/news-and-updates/all-news?search=&topicCategoryFilters=&programCategoryFilters=&newsTypeFilters=1061c992a3f944fca4b48447c8185fa9&divisionFilters=&sort=&activeFilters=newstypes%7C&page=1&industryFilters=&utilityFilters=&sectionFilters=&publicationFilters=)
New filings are added to ongoing and new proceedings that impact California’s utility services, communities, and consumers, many of which are open for public comment. These filings are available to view on a weekly basis. 
![Man looking at the PRA Request website on a desktop computer](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/news-and-outreach/images/pracomputer1.jpg?mw=645&hash=FDF9805768A3E121D40F1F3175E6B4A3)
## [ Public Records Act Requests ](https://cpuc.nextrequest.com/)
Make a Public Records Act request or search through requests that have already been made to the CPUC.

##  How can we help?
Emergency? **Call 911**

#  Rolling Portfolio Program Guidance
**Background**
The Commission established and adopted the Rolling Portfolio process for reviewing the utilities energy efficiency program administrator's portfolios in October 2015. In August 2016, the commission adopted D.16-08-019, which provided changes to requirements for statewide and third party programs and their administration. Under the context of the energy efficiency rolling portfolio overseen by the investor-owned utilities, D.18-01-004, approved the solicitation process for procurement of energy efficiency programs for the next several years and beyond. The decision requires the utilities to have at least 60 percent of their energy efficiency portfolio budgets designed and implemented by third party implementers by the end of 2022. This page will contain guidance for programs as well as the third party solicitation.
  * Business Plan Guidance 
    * [D.16-08-019](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M166/K232/166232537.docx "D.16-08-019")
    * [D.21-05-031](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M385/K864/385864616.PDF)
      * [Attachment A Guidance – Finalized July 15, 2021](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/caeecc_final-revision_attachment-a_clean.pdf)
    * 2024-2031 Business Plan Templates
      * [Program closure/Launch checklist](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/posted-program-closure-checklist-required-by-d2105031-12312021.docx)
      * [EE Business Plan and Application Template](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/ee-business-plan-and-application-template---final-from-ed-with-emv.docx)
      * (Outdated) [2024-2031 EE Application Attachment Tables](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/2024-2031-ee-application-attachment-tables.xlsx)
      * [Revised 2024-2031 EE Application Attachment Tables with Segment Metrics](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/2024-2031-ee-application-attachment-tables_w-seg-metrics.xlsx)
        * [Attachment B Supplemental Budget Information](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/attachment-b-supplemental-budget-information-narrative-template_modified.docx)
    * 2028-2035 Business Plan Templates 
      * [EE Business Plan Narrative Template](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/bpa-narrative02042026.docx) (February 2026) 
      * [EE Business Plan Workbook Template](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/bpa-workbook030226.xlsx) (March 2026)
  * Phasing Out Natural Gas EE Incentives 
    * [Staff Proposal](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/ng-staff-prop-81622.pdf)
    * Staff Proposal Workshop: August 26, 2022 
      * [Workshop slides](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/ng-staff-proposal-826-workshop-pres.pdf)
      * [Workshop recording](https://cpuc.webex.com/cpuc/ldr.php?RCID=d9537e3bb2c72a12cf61f2fa369a1ee8) (password: CkYS3Qdj)
  * Competitive Solicitation Guidance 
    * [D.16-12-036](http://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M171/K555/171555623.docx "D.16-12-036")
    * [Innovation Definition for Third-Party Programs](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/energy-efficiency-innovation-definition-for-third-party-programs.pdf)
    * [Integrated Demand Side-Management (IDSM) Policy References](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/energy-efficiency-integrated-demand-side-management-idsm-policy-references.pdf)
  * Programs and Projects Using Normalized Metered Energy Consumption (NMEC)  

    * [NMEC Rulebook](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/nmec-rulebook-21-march-2025.pdf) (**Revised January 2025)**
    * [Reporting](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/nmec-reporting-guidance_04242020.pdf)
    * [](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/nmec-reporting-guidance_04242020.pdf)[Strategic Energy Management Program](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/RollingPortfolioPgmGuidance/CA_Ind_SEM_Design_Guide.pdf "Strategic Energy Management Program")
  * Technical Guidance 
    * [Site-level NMEC Technical Guidance](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/l/6442463695-lbnl-nmec-techguidance-01072020.pdf "Site-level NMEC Technical Guidance")
    * [Weighted Average Expected Useful Life/Net to Gross Method](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/RollingPortfolioPgmGuidance/Combining_Measures_Claims.DRAFT.xlsm "Weighted Average Expected Useful Life/Net to Gross Method")
    * [SEM Measurement and Verification (M&V)](https://files.cpuc.ca.gov/gopher-data/energy_division/EnergyEfficiency/RollingPortfolioPgmGuidance/CA_Industrial_SEM_MV_Guide.pdf "SEM Measurement and Verification \(M&amp;V\)")
    * HAAS Experimental Design and RCT(Posted 5/1/2018) 
      * [Large Population Level Energy Efficiency Program Design Method Checklist](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/e/6442457319-e2e-project-3rd-party-energy-efficiency-evaluation-checklist.pdf "Large Population Level Energy Efficiency Program Design Method Checklist")
      * Randomized Controlled [Trial: Design and Implementation](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/legacyfiles/r/6442457320-randomized-controlled-trial.pdf "RandomizedControlled Trial: Design and Implementation")
  * Implementation Plans 
    * [Implementation Plan Template Guidance](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/energy-efficiency-implementation-plan-template-ver-31-dec-2025.docx) (Dec 2025)
    * [Implementation Plan Change Summary Form](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/rolling-portfolio-program-guidance/energy-efficiency-implementation-plan-change-summary-form-template-ver-31-dec-2025.docx) (Dec 2025)

  2. [Proceedings and Rulemaking](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking)


#  Proceedings and Rulemaking
We regulate utility companies and infrastructure in California.
##  Featured Links
### [ Docket Card ](http://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=401:1:0)
Find information and documents for a specific proceeding. 
### [ Follow a Proceeding ](http://subscribecpuc.cpuc.ca.gov/)
Subscribe to documents published for formal proceedings, resolutions, agenda related materials, and press releases. Follow a particular proceeding, industry, or type of document. 
### [ Participate in a Proceeding ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/cpuc-public-participation-hearings/methods-for-becoming-a-party-to-a-proceeding)
Learn how you can become a party to a proceeding, which depends on what stage the proceeding is in. 
### [ E-File a Document ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/e-file-a-document)
Electronic filing lets you send documents to the CPUC to be processed and included with other pleadings in formal proceedings. 
### [ Locate documents ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/locate-documents)
Including Decisions and Proposed Decision (as well as red-lined revisions) Official CPUC-generated proceeding documents, Advice Letters, electronically filed documents, staff reports, press releases, environmental impact reports and more. 
### [ Intervenor Compensation ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/intervenor-compensation)
Providing financial resources to individuals and groups representing residential or small commercial electric utility customers to bring their concerns and interests during formal proceedings. 
### [ Daily Calendar ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/events-and-meetings)
Daily calendar includes today’s events. 
### [ Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/alternative-dispute-resolution)
Processes, such as facilitation, negotiation, mediation, and/or early neutral evaluation, help customers resolve a conflict without a court or agency’s formal decision. 
### [ Practitioner’s Page ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/administrative-law-judge/practitioners-page)
Find all the information you need for both new and veteran practitioners in one place. 
### [ Appealing Citations ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/administrative-law-judge/appealing-citations)
General information and instruction on how to appeal citations. 
### [ Decisions & Resolutions re: CPUC Practice ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/decisions-and-resolutions-related-to-practice-before-the-cpuc)
Documents creating other requirements as they relate to practice and procedure at CPUC that are not part of the Rules of Practice and Procedure or General Orders. 
### [ Get Involved  ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/cpuc-public-participation-hearings/get-involved)
Learn about hearings, events, webcasts, ordering documents, and other ways you can participate in the processes of CPUC. 
##  General Rate Cases
General rate cases (GRCs) are proceedings used to address the costs of operating and maintaining the utility system and the allocation of those costs among customer classes.
[Learn more](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/electric-rates/general-rate-case)
##  More Information
### [ CPUC Public Participation Hearings ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/cpuc-public-participation-hearings)
Hearings and other events to hear from the public on issues before the CPUC. 
### [ Tracking Proceedings/Joining Service Lists ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/divisions/news-and-public-information-office/public-advisors-office/tracking-issues-of-interest)
Learn how to follow proceedings covering issues of interest to you, including how to find CPUC documents, and information on meetings and hearings. 
### [ Creating Formal Documents - Templates Provided  ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/cpuc-public-participation-hearings/creating-formal-documents---templates-provided)
All formal documents must follow formatting guidelines, learn how to make sure your documents meet all the requirements. 
### [ Serving and Filing Proceeding Documents  ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/proceedings-and-rulemaking/cpuc-public-participation-hearings/serving-and-filing-proceeding-documents)
If you have created your formal documents, the next step is to serve them on the proceeding’s service list and file them with the CPUC Docket Office. 
### [ Glossary ](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/news-and-updates/newsroom/glossary)
Glossary of Acronyms and Other Frequently Used Terms 
##  Looking for a Specific Type of Document?
Different types of documents and files require a special type of search functionality. Explore advanced search options to retrieve a specific document. 
  * [ Advanced Search ](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/advancedsearchform.aspx)
  * [ Advice Letters Search ](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=505:1:0::NO::P1_BRANCH,P1_TITLE:W,Water)
  * [ Advice Letters Statistics ](https://apps.cpuc.ca.gov/apex/f?p=404:3:0:::::)
  * [ Agenda Decisions ](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/aDecisionsSearchForm.aspx)
  * [ Agenda Search ](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/AgendaSearchForm.aspx)

#  Valid Survey
The CPUC ensures the provision of safe, reliable utility service and infrastructure through the regulation of privately owned electric, natural gas, telecommunications, water, railroad, rail transit, and passenger transportation companies. On this page you will find valid surveys sent to you by authorized provider/administrator. 
Updated: March 24, 2026
**Authorized Provider /Administrator** | **Delivering Method** | **Subject Keywords** | **Active Dates and Where Survey Emails Will Originate From** | **CPUC Contact**  
---|---|---|---|---  
Guidehouse |  Email and text message (from Qualtrics) | Household Energy Upgrades Survey  
  
Energy Savings Assistance (ESA)  
  
California Alternate Rates for Energy (CARE)  
  
Family Electric Rate Assistance Program (FERA) |  April 2026 Emails and text messages originating from the Qualtrics surveys platform, administered by Guidehouse |  kapil.kulkarni@cpuc.ca.gov  
Guidehouse and Quantum Analytics |  Phone and Web Surveys (Qualtrics) |  Heat Pump Water  
Heater Study Energy-Efficiency  
Starter Kit Tankless Water Heaters  |  Active between November 2025 - July 2026 Phone calls made Quantum Analytics staff Emails originating from the Qualtrics surveys platform, administered by Guidehouse |  melissa.matis@cpuc.ca.gov  
Opinion Dynamics  | Qualtrics Survey via email |  GoGreen Home Energy (GoGreen Home) Financing. Sent to participants who received a microloan or standard loan through GGH, and to a small selection of non-participants | "Provide Feedback Regarding Your GoGreen Home Financing Loan.” Emails come from @opiniondynamics.com. In the field, November 2025-January 2026 |  kevin.feizi@cpuc.ca.gov  
Opinion Dynamics  | Email (from Qualtrics)  | Codes and standards, codes, code compliance, Compliance program  | From October 2025  | Taylor.Williams@opiniondynamics.com  
DNV Energy and Quantum Energy | Email and telephone  |  Commercial Industrial and Agricultural Custom (CIAC), Savings By Design (SBD) PG&E, SCE, SDGE, MCE, SoCalREN, BayREN |  Active survey dates: October 2025 – January 2026 DNV Energy will conduct customer outreach (surveys) by email and telephone. Recruitment emails and calendar invites may come from users using the @dnv.com domain. |  leanne.hoadley@cpuc.ca.gov  
DNV Energy & Verdant Assoc., LLP |  Residential tenants and trainees surveys: DNV Energy will conduct customer outreach (surveys) via a Qualitrics web survey. Verdant Associates will field surveys with property managers and contractors by telephone.  |  Solar on Multifamily Affordable Housing (SOMAH) Program |  Active survey dates: October 2025 – January 2026 |  sarah.lerhaupt@cpuc.ca.gov  
DNV Energy | Email and phone call outreach to site-level NMEC participants. |  Site-level NMEC PY2024 Evaluation, gross and net interview  PG&E, SCE, SDG&E, IREN, SoCalREN |  2025/10/15 (assumed, pending workplan approval)-2026/1/16 Email and phone calls from DNV. Outreach will be primarily by phone and the survey will be conducted by DNV Energy employees. Recruitment emails and calendar invites may come from users using the @dnv.com domain. |  christina.torok@cpuc.ca.gov  
DNV Energy |  Email and phone call outreach to population NMEC participants. |  Population NMEC PY2022-2024 Evaluation, gross and net interview PGE, SCE, SDGE, SCG, MCE, BayREN, 3CREN |  2025/10/15 (assumed, pending workplan approval)-2026/1/16 Email and phone calls from DNV. Outreach will be primarily by phone and the survey will be conducted by DNV Energy employees. Recruitment emails and calendar invites may come from users using the @dnv.com domain. | peng.gong@cpuc.ca.gov  
Verdant Associates, LLC and DNV  
|  Phone or Email to participants and non-participants, including solar contractors and residential tenants. |  Solar on Multifamily Affordable Homes (SOMAH) Evaluation |  March 1, 2025 until December 31, 2025 – Emails and phone calls from Verdant and DNV staff  
|  sarah.lerhaupt@cpuc.ca.ca.gov Current Customer Generation Program Evaluations: <https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/customer-generation-evaluation>  
Verdant Associates | Web Surveys (Qualtrics)  
|  Heat Pump Water Heater Study  “Share you experience with your Heat Pump Water Heater” |  Active between Dec 2024 – Jan 2026 Emails from verdant@qualtrics-research.com Emails/Phone Calls from Verdant Associates and kW Engineering Staff | garima.chatrath@cpuc.ca.gov  
Verdant Associates, LLC |  Web survey through Qualtrics email distribution system  
|  Disadvantaged Communities – Single-Family Solar Homes (DAC-SASH) |  September 2025 – October 2025 Emails from verdant@qualtrics-research.com  
|  tory.francisco@cpuc.ca.gov  
|  Email |  Sent to CA residents, 18+, signed up for YouGov's panel, Flex Alert Tracking Survey "Share your opinions in a YouGov Survey" |  yougov@yougov.com |  [erik.johnson.cpuc.ca.gov](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/erik.johnson.cpuc.ca.gov)

#  CPUC General Orders
General Orders translated into other languages
**General Order** |  **Effective  
Version** |  **Description**  
---|---|---  
22 |  [22-B](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PUBLISHED/Graphics/619.PDF) |  Reports of accidents on railroads  
24 |  [24-C](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M026/K563/26563266.PDF) |  Supersedes General Order 24-B (effective July 1, 1964). General Order 24-C became effective on August 27, 2012. Revisions to General Order 24 include: 1) the filing of a General Order 24 report on a quarterly instead of a monthly basis for the first year of the Financing Rule. Commencing in the second year, reports will be filed semi-annually (June and December); 2) revisions to the type of information provided in such reports; 3) elimination of requirement that a utility maintain a separate bank account to record money derived from the sale of securities, except where the Commission specifically designates what the proceeds can be used for, or in instances where the loan will be repaid by surcharge; and 4) an updated list of information required in the report, given the manner in which securities transactions are now recorded by utilities as required by other regulatory entities.

168 |  [168](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M089/K440/89440106.PDF) |  Consumer Bill of Rights Governing Telecommunications Services. Market Rules to Empower Telecommunications Consumers and to Prevent Fraud.  
169 |  [169](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M155/K779/155779569.PDF "169") |  Implementing the Digital Infrastructure and Video Competition Act of 2006 (DIVCA)  
171 |  [171](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PUBLISHED/GRAPHICS/133206.PDF) |  Rules Governing Filings Made Pursuant to the Telecommunications Act of 1996  
172 |  [172](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PUBLISHED/GENERAL_ORDER/146010.htm) |  Rules And Regulations Governing The Use Of Personal Electronic Devices By Employees Of Rail Transit Agencies And Rail Fixed Guideway Systems  
173 |  [173](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M027/K106/27106129.PDF "General Order #173") |  Regarding advice letter approval of certain transfers of interests in utility property pursuant to Public Utilities Code Section 851  
174 |  [174](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M538/K277/538277677.pdf) |  Rules for Electric Utility Substations Check [here](https://ia.cpuc.ca.gov/gos/GO174/GO174_startup_page.html) for historical information.  
175A  |  [175-A](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M159/K905/159905345.pdf "175-A") | Rules and Regulations Governing Roadway Worker Protection provided by Rail Transit Agencies and Rail fixed Guideway Systems  
176 |  [176](https://docs.cpuc.ca.gov/PublishedDocs/Published/G000/M151/K399/151399809.pdf) | Rules for Overhead 25 kV Railroad Electrification Systems for a High-Speed Rail System  
177 |  [177](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/administrative-law-judge-division/documents/general-orders/go_177_gas_infrastructure.pdf) |  Planning and construction review for certain gas system infrastructure

  3. [Commissioners](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/commissioners)


#  Commissioners
Learn about the Governor appointed Commissioners serving on the CPUC. 
The Governor appoints the five Commissioners, who must be confirmed by the Senate, for six year staggered terms. The Governor appoints one of the five to serve as Commission President.
  * See the Commissioners’ [strategic directives and governance policies](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/files/uploadedfiles/cpuc_public_website/content/about_us/mission_and_values/strategic-directives-and-governance-policies.pdf "Strategic Directives and Governance Policies").
  * See [all Commissioners](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/commissioners/commissioners-timeline_030626.pdf) appointed since 1911.
  * Commissioners’ Form 803 Behested Payment Reports pursuant to Government Code section 82015

  

![John Reynolds, CPUC President](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/commissioners/john-reynolds/images/john-reynolds.jpg?mw=645&hash=54181BECE7A0A5FBB3E649CB017959E6)

  3. [Consultant and contract opportunities](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/about-cpuc/consultant-and-contract-opportunities)


#  Consultant and Contract Opportunities
Discover the consulting and contract opportunities available with us and how you can bid. 
**Contracting Opportunities can now be found on the State of California’s E-Procure website.** Opportunities that the commission currently has available will be listed below and will include the name of the opportunity, the “Event ID” number and the end date.
To download a copy of the opportunity, you must [visit the E-procurement website](https://caleprocure.ca.gov/pages/index.aspx) and search for bids. You can search for the opportunity, by using one (or all) of the following criteria and clicking the search button:
  * Event ID -- shown in the listing below
  * Event Name -- the contract number (just the RFP/RFQ portion) 
  * Department -- enter 8660


It is the policy of the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) to promote and encourage Small Business (SB) and Disabled Veteran Business Enterprise (DVBE) participation in all procurement and contracting activities. CPUC offers SB preferences and DVBE incentives to certified bidders. Go to **[OSDS Website](https://www.dgs.ca.gov/PD/Services/Page-Content/Procurement-Division-Services-List-Folder/Apply-for-or-Re-apply-as-Small-Business-Disabled-Veteran-Business-Enterprise)** to learn about California’s Small Business (SB) and Disabled Veteran Business Enterprise (DVBE) certification program
All CPUC solicitations are posted to the State of California’s **[Cal e-Procure Website](https://caleprocure.ca.gov/pages/Events-BS3/event-search.aspx)**. CPUC recommends all suppliers and bidders become registered on Cal e-Procure. You can learn how to register here: **[Sell to the State](https://caleprocure.ca.gov/pages/bidder-vendor.aspx)**
The Cal e-Procure system includes an advertisement feature. Suppliers and bidders can place ads on a solicitation to promote themselves and find subcontractors or prime contractors to partner with in submitting a solicitation response. To place adds you must be registered on Cal e-Procure. Once you have logged in, click Post Vendor Ad to get started. The following link has more information on accessing bid solicitations and placing ads. **[Learn how to access bid solicitations and place ads](https://youtu.be/m8NrKwl2z9g)**
##  More In This Section

[California States Contract Register](http://caleprocure.ca.gov/)
